FIX (c0432862): use `get_data_ptr(&name)` instead of `src_slot.ptr` -- it loads-through one ref-level for `ref`/`mut ref` params and unboxes RC-promoted bindings past the refcount header, while owned bindings still return `slot.ptr` unchanged (common case untouched). Test e2e_vec_clone_borrowed_receiver_is_deep (codegen): a `mut ref` snapshot taken at a recursion leaf survives the unwind pops, and a `ref`-borrow snapshot is independent of a later source drain. codegen 1618 / memory_sanitizer ASAN 264 green; macOS ASAN covers the double-free/UAF, Linux LSan the leak class.

A `Vec.sort_by(|a, b| a.cmp(b))` whose enclosing function got AUTO-PARALLELIZED failed LLVM module verification ('Referring to an argument in another function!  %mcall.cancel.flag = load i8, ptr %1' + 'Function return type does not match operand type of return inst! ret void / i64'). When the auto-par pass turns a function's statements into `par {}` branches, `branch_cancel_ptr` points at the current par-branch fn's `cancel_flag` argument so mid-branch method calls can cooperatively cancel. But the sort_by lowering then emits SEPARATE C-ABI helper functions -- the monomorphized insertion-sort routine `__vec_<elem>_sort_by_mono_N(ptr,i64)` and the comparator thunk `__sort_by_inline_N(ptr,ptr,ptr)` -- whose bodies were compiled WITHOUT clearing `branch_cancel_ptr`. The comparator's `a.cmp(b)` ran `emit_branch_cancel_check('mcall', ...)`, which emitted `load i8, ptr %1` against the stale pointer; but `%1` in those helpers is an element pointer / the i64 length, not a cancel flag -- the instruction referenced an argument of a different function, and the cancel-branch's `ret void` landed in the i64-returning thunk. `karac run` and `KARAC_AUTO_PAR=0 karac build` were both fine; only the auto-par default build failed. Surfaced by kata:39: the sorted solver (combination_sum_sorted.kara) sorts the candidates, and a `path.clone()` later in the same function tipped the auto-par analyzer into parallelizing the (disjoint) sort and clone groups. The general `compile_closure` had the same latent gap (any closure body compiled under a set cancel ptr). FIX (c0432862): take()/restore `branch_cancel_ptr` around each separate-function body emission -- `compile_closure` (src/codegen/closures.rs), `emit_sort_by_inline_thunk`, `emit_sort_by_key_inline_thunk`, `emit_sort_by_mono` (src/codegen/vec_method.rs) -- mirroring the par/reduce/task_group/mono emitters that already do so. Test test_auto_par_sort_by_comparator_no_stray_cancel_flag_e2e (par_codegen): an auto-par'd function that sorts one vec and independently builds+clones another (so the analyzer actually parallelizes) now builds and prints the right combined result. par_codegen 122 / codegen 1618 / parallax green.

`Vec.from_slice(arr[a..b])` / `try_from_slice` with a RANGE-slice source failed codegen ('Vec.from_slice: nested-index source `arr[i]` requires outer to be Vec[Vec[T]]'). recover_from_slice_src (src/codegen/assoc_call.rs) routed EVERY ExprKind::Index source to the scalar nested-index Vec[Vec[T]] path (rows[r]), never distinguishing a Range index (arr[a..b], which yields a slice header exactly like a bare slice/vec/array Identifier) from a scalar element index. So the natural 'copy a window of an array/vec into an owned Vec' shape -- e.g. read N bytes into a buffer then Vec.from_slice(buf[0..n]) to hand to a parser -- was a hard codegen error. Surfaced by the Relay dogfood (slice 4 path routing) bridging a read buffer to String.from_utf8 (which takes Vec[u8]). FIX: recover_from_slice_src now matches `Index { object, index } if index is Range` as a range-slice -- recovers the element type from the outer array/vec/slice var and lowers via coerce_to_slice (the same compile_range_slice path a bare f(arr[a..b]) call argument uses); a scalar nested index still takes the Vec[Vec[T]] path. A use_coerce flag drives the (data,len) recovery. Regression: tests/codegen.rs::e2e_from_slice_range_of_array_codegen (Vec.from_slice(arr[1..4]) of Array[i64,5] -> Vec[20,30,40]). Green: codegen suite, ASAN 266, fmt+clippy.

An `Array[T, N]` passed to a `ref Slice[T]` parameter mis-built (segfault / vec-index-out-of-bounds) while the interpreter ran correctly -- a run/build divergence. Root cause: `extract_slice_elem_type` (src/codegen/types_lowering.rs) returned None through a `TypeKind::Ref` wrapper, so `ref Slice[T]` was classified as a BARE ref param (slice_elems=None, ref_flags=true). At the regular call site (src/codegen/call_dispatch.rs) the `is_ref` + identifier fast-path passed `get_data_ptr(array)` -- the array's RAW ELEMENT STORAGE -- as the slice argument, so the callee read `{ptr,len}` out of the array's first two elements (ptr=elem0, len=elem1): a bogus slice header -> OOB/segfault. A `Vec` source accidentally survived (its `{ptr,len,cap}` storage starts with `{ptr,len}`); a `Slice`/`ref Slice` binding's get_data_ptr already yields a header pointer; only an `Array`'s storage lacks the header. `ref Slice[T]` is a DOCUMENTED param mode (design.md Slices, `fn average[T: Numeric](xs: ref Slice[T])`). Surfaced by kata:41 (first-missing-positive) while probing read-only slice param forms for the seen-array solver (which ships with the idiomatic bare `Slice[i64]`, unaffected). FIX (6d207a7e): (1) extract_slice_elem_type peels one Ref/MutRef around a Slice/MutSlice so `ref Slice[T]` reports its element type (a non-slice inner -> still None); (2) the call path, for a `ref Slice` param whose ARGUMENT is an Array binding, synthesizes the {ptr,len} header via coerce_to_slice and passes a pointer to it (materialize_rvalue_for_ref_arg) -- the shape the rvalue-ref path already uses for `v.as_slice()`. Narrowed to Array sources so a forwarded ref-slice binding keeps using get_data_ptr (re-coercing it corrupted the forward, caught + fixed). Test e2e_array_arg_to_ref_slice_param (direct Array->ref Slice + ref-slice forwarding + ref-slice forward with a Vec[bool] indexed by a slice value). codegen 1618 / memory_sanitizer ASAN 264 + Linux LSan slice 17 / par_codegen 121 green. NOTE: the regular sync call path is fixed; the coroutine/mono/method-call paths share the same is_ref-vs-slice logic and a `ref Slice`-from-Array argument there is not covered by this change (rare; not exercised by the kata).

FIXED: `String.from_utf8(v: Vec[u8]) -> Result[String, Utf8Error]` was interpreter-only; the `Result.Ok(line)` String binding failed CODEGEN with 'Undefined variable'. Now wired through codegen: a `compile_string_from_utf8` lowering extracts the input Vec's {data,len} and delegates to a new shared `build_utf8_validated_result` helper (extracted from `compile_cstr_to_string`, which already built Result[String, Utf8Error] for CStr.to_string), reusing the `karac_runtime_cstr_to_string` validator (validates UTF-8 + COPIES the bytes into a fresh heap String on Ok; writes the Utf8Error discriminant on Err). Consume-by-copy (like Vec.push(param)) so the input Vec's own scope-exit drop frees its buffer — no ownership transfer. Dispatched in assoc_call.rs (String.from_utf8). No new runtime code. Surfaced by + unblocks the Relay slice-4 path-routing parse (read bytes -> Vec[u8] -> from_utf8 -> split/starts_with -> route). Regression: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_string_from_utf8_result (Ok ASCII + Err InvalidByte + Err IncompleteSequence) and test_e2e_request_line_routing_parse (the full request-line parse + route composition). Green: codegen suite, ASAN, fmt+clippy.

A heap value (String/Vec[T]) moved into a spawn/tg.spawn closure was LEAKED once per spawn when the closure body only BORROWED it. Completes B-2026-06-18-8: that fix suppressed the parent's scope-exit FreeVecBuffer for the capture (the {data,len,cap} header is bitwise-copied into the task's heap env, so the parent must not free a buffer the task still reads -- closing a loop use-after-free), but it ONLY suppressed -- it never re-homed the buffer to a new owner. The spawn wrapper (lower_spawn_shared, src/codegen/task_group.rs) binds each captured free var into a fresh wrapper-local alloca but registered scope-exit cleanup ONLY for channel-end captures (track_channel_var), never for heap builtins. So when the closure body merely BORROWS the capture -- check(addr) whose `addr: String` param is inferred `ref`, the canonical `loop { let s = base.clone(); tg.spawn(|| use(s)) }` server-handler shape -- the body consumes nothing and the parent is suppressed, so NOTHING frees the buffer: one leaked clone per spawn (LSan: N x karac_string_clone reachable from main). Invisible on macOS (no LeakSanitizer; ASAN sees only the double-free/UAF the suppress-only fix already cured), so it passed local macOS ASAN while leaking under the authoritative Linux LSan gate (scripts/lsan-local.sh) -- the exact residual the B-2026-06-18-8 ledger note deferred to 'the Linux LSan CI gate's job'. Pre-existing on clean main (b96c773b); both coro and non-coro spawn paths affected (shared capture loop); platform-agnostic (not cfg-gated). FIX (9a5e1c39): lower_spawn_shared now re-registers the parent's exact FreeVecBuffer (same elem_ty / elem_is_tensor / elem_map_drop / elem_agg_drop payload, matched by the capture's PARENT alloca exactly as the channel-end transfer is) against the WRAPPER-LOCAL alloca, pushed onto the wrapper-scope cleanup frame and drained by drain_top_frame_with_emit at task exit (before the env struct free). The task now frees the buffer at completion -- the ownership hand-off the suppression always implied. If the body instead MOVES the capture into an owning callee, the standard move path zeros the source cap, so the transferred cleanup's `cap > 0` guard skips it: exactly one free, no double-free. Mirrors the existing user-Drop (suppress_user_drop_for_var) and channel-end (track_channel_var/suppress_channel_drop_for_var) capture-ownership transfers; same family as B-2026-06-18-8 and B-2026-06-17-9 (a moved-into-spawn value freed by the wrong owner). Regression (tests/memory_sanitizer.rs, all under scripts/lsan-local.sh): asan_taskgroup_spawn_heap_capture_in_loop_{coro,noncoro}_no_uaf now pass under LSan (previously leaked 6x30B from main, while passing on macOS); new asan_taskgroup_spawn_heap_capture_moved_into_callee_single_free guards the no-double-free half (capture moved into a local Vec inside the task -- exactly one free). Green: full memory_sanitizer LSan suite 270/270, fmt + clippy clean.

The self-hosted parser leaked its returned AST node: render_expr(parse_expr(src)) on input "1" leaked 80 bytes (the `shared enum Expr` box, allocated in Parser.parse_expr_bp) under Linux ASAN+LSan. PRE-EXISTING and independent of #35; the parser oracle (tests/selfhost_parser.rs) never caught it because the karac-built parser binary is not ASAN-instrumented. Root cause: a bare `shared` (RC) enum/struct value produced as a function-return (or inline variant-ctor) TEMPORARY and passed BY VALUE to a consumer (render_expr(e: Expr)) was never freed. A bare-shared by-value param is NET-ZERO -- the callee emit_refcount_incs at entry and track_rc_var-decs at exit (the caller-keeps-reference convention, functions.rs) -- so the caller still owns the temp's +1; a let-bound producer (let e = parse(...); render(e)) drops it via the binding's scope-exit dec, but a directly-passed temporary has no binding, so the box leaked once per call (a deep parse leaks the whole tree). The fresh-temp-arg materialization beside it in call_dispatch.rs (#20) was Vec/String-only on the belief the callee balances the ref -- true for Option[shared T] (callee-consumed via track_rc_option_var), false for a bare shared box. FIX: compile_call's arg loop now queues the caller-side dec for a fresh bare-shared box arg -- fresh_arg_bare_shared_heap_type resolves the box heap layout from the producing call's return type (fn_return_type_names, or enum_name_of_expr for a variant ctor) in shared_types, then track_rc_var("__owned_arg_tmp", ...). A passthrough guard (skip when the producing call has its own fresh shared-box arg) keeps a g(make()) chain from registering two decs against one box (double-free), leaving exactly the innermost producer's dec. Confirmed via scripts/lsan-local.sh: input "1" and deep parses (1+2*3-4/5, nested unary, tuples) all clean; selfhost_parser oracle still matches the Rust parser. Regression test asan_shared_enum_fnret_temp_arg_freed_no_leak (recursive build/render in a loop, all-scalar payloads, >=48-byte boxes). memory_sanitizer 268, codegen 1628, par_codegen 122 green. The orthogonal whole-struct-payload-binding String-field leak is B-2026-06-19-4.

fix: 8a78ee6d (phase-12 #41) — implemented ledger candidate #2: the shared-enum box rc-drop now WALKS a plain-struct payload that owns a String/Vec/heap field (emit_shared_enum_field_drop + field_is_walkable now gate on `|| type_expr_has_drop_heap(te)`, and emit_nested_struct_shared_rc_decs gained a direct-String cap-guarded free under owns_buffer_free). The double-free tension is resolved by the binding side: bind_pattern_values deliberately does NOT track_struct_var a whole-struct binding `n` for a shared-enum scrutinee (pattern_binding.rs `!pattern_binding_scrutinee_is_shared_enum`), so the box rc-drop is the SOLE owner of name's buffer — exactly one free, no leak and no double-free, regardless of whether the arm reads or consumes `n`. Verified LSan-clean for BOTH the recursive consuming case (asan_shared_enum_recursive_struct_payload_string_freed_no_leak) and the ledger's exact read-only single-level repro (asan_shared_enum_struct_variant_whole_binding_readonly_no_leak, added here).
title: A `match` arm that binds a `shared enum` struct-variant payload as a WHOLE struct (Int(n) / Ident(n), then reads n.suffix / n.name) does not drop the bound struct's String/Vec fields at arm-scope exit, so identifier/string AST nodes leak their text (the IdentExpr.name / StrLit.value buffer) -- e.g. the self-hosted parser on a long identifier leaks ~41 bytes/parse under Linux LSan, even after B-2026-06-19-3 freed the enclosing box. The box's rc-drop emits a plain free (emit_shared_enum_field_drop returns false for a struct payload owning a String but no shared field -- it only walks struct payloads that own SHARED fields, or direct Vec/String payloads), and the whole-struct binding `n` is a shallow copy whose String fields are never registered for drop, so neither owner frees the buffer. Input "1" (empty suffix) and all-scalar payloads (Span) are unaffected -- this is purely the String/Vec-bearing struct-variant-bound-as-whole case. Not fixed in B-2026-06-19-3 (scoped to the box): the safe fix has a double-free tension (the shallow-copy `n` aliases the box payload; exactly one owner must free it), the codebase's 'better to leak than double-free' policy left it. Two candidate fixes: make the match-arm whole-struct binding own+drop its heap fields (n takes the moved-out payload; box stays plain-free), OR make the box rc-drop walk the struct payload's String/Vec (requires the box to exclusively own the payload, which the aliased `n` violates). Repro shape: shared enum E { V(S) } struct S { name: String } ; render(e: E) { match e { V(n) => n.name.len() } }, called as render(make()) in a loop with a >=36-byte name.

A computed scalar pushed/stored into a sub-word-element collection (Vec[u8] / Vec[bool] / Vec[u16] / Vec[u32], and the slice index-store) was stored at FULL i64 width over the 1/2/4-byte slot, smearing up to 7 bytes past each element. Harmless inside allocation slack, but the push that fills an exact-size-class buffer (cap 64/128/256...) overflowed the allocation by those trailing bytes, corrupting the adjacent heap -> an ASLR-intermittent SIGSEGV on a later realloc/free once the buffer grew past ~64 elements. The pushed values read back CORRECT (consecutive overlapping wide stores each preserve their own low byte, and the final element's high bytes are zero), so it ran clean under the interpreter and printed the right answer in small programs; only large sub-word Vecs built with COMPUTED elements (e.g. `v.push(b'a' + (i as u8))`, the kata-44 wildcard bench terrain) crashed. ASAN MISSED it (the 7-byte spill lands in the allocator's realloc-rounding slack, not a poisoned redzone), so the whole memory_sanitizer suite passed while `karac build` binaries segfaulted -- the guard had to be build+run E2E, not the sanitizer. Root cause: the element build_store in vec_method.rs (push / try_push / push_front / try_push_front) and collections.rs (compile_vec_index_store / compile_slice_index_store) wrote `elem_val` raw; the GEP and allocation correctly use the 1-byte elem stride/size, but a u8-typed expression that involves arithmetic or a cast compiles to i64, so the store width (driven by the value's LLVM type) was 8. Same bug class as the struct-field 8-bytes-over-a-1-byte-field overflow already cured by coerce_to_struct_field_ty (2026-06-06). FIX (66a489ef): coerce the value to the element type via coerce_scalar_to_type (truncate when wider) immediately before each of the six element build_store sites. Regression (tests/codegen.rs, build+run): e2e_subword_vec_push_narrows_to_elem_width_no_heap_overflow (240 computed-u8 pushes crossing the 64/128 cap boundaries + a computed index-store) and e2e_subword_vec_bool_push_no_heap_overflow; both reliably crashed pre-fix. Green: codegen 1619, par_codegen 121, memory_sanitizer 264, fmt + clippy clean.

A read-only `let r = v[i]` binding of a HEAP-owning element out of a `Vec[Vec[T]]` (or any Vec whose element type is non-trivially-copyable) ALWAYS deep-clones the element, even when `r` is only read and never escapes — doubling allocation traffic on enumerate-then-scan workloads. Found via kata #46 (permutations): the n!-enumeration bench ran ~2x slower than C, and a malloc interposer pinned it to Kara issuing EXACTLY 2x C's allocations (10,080 vs 5,040 mallocs/solve at n=7). Decomposition: the append `out.push(path.clone())` is a correct SINGLE-alloc move (the clone buffer is moved in, source cap zeroed); the redundant second malloc+free per element is the signature loop's `let perm = perms[j]` — `clone_owned_vec_index_element` (collections.rs ~1478, called from the let arm in stmts.rs ~1779) deep-clones the shallow element so the binding owns a distinct buffer, guarding the alias double-free / dangling-after-container-drop class (B-2026-06-14-11, B-2026-06-18-9, the self-hosted-parser UAF). Confirmed: rewriting the read as a direct double-index `perms[j][i]` (no element binding) drops to 1 malloc/elem and matches C (verified: bound form 200,030 mallocs / direct form 100,030 over 100k iters). The clone is SEMANTICALLY required (an element cannot be moved out of a Vec by indexing) — eliminating it is an observational-equivalence optimization that needs a borrow-safety proof, so it sits at the documented v1 perf bar (design.md: 'within ~2x apples-to-apples') and is the roadmap's #1 lever ('allocation reduction on hot paths'). PLANNED FIX (clone-elision): at the `let r = v[i]` site, bind `r` as a shallow borrow (alias the element buffer) and suppress r's scope-exit FreeVecBuffer, gated on a conservative whitelist lexical scan over r's scope (rest of enclosing block + nested blocks) proving (a) r is an immutable simple binding, (b) the element type is heap-owning, (c) r appears only in read positions — index-read, `.len()`/`.is_empty()`, never a call/own-arg/return/assign/non-read-method/composite-literal (the use-classifier's never-Consumed signal corroborates), (d) the container v appears only as index-reads in r's scope (no mutating method, assign, index-store, by-value move — any v.push reallocs and would dangle r), (e) no closure literal in r's scope. Any uncertainty falls back to the existing clone (false-negatives are free; only false-positives are UAF, so the gate is whitelist-only). Regression must include adversarial NEGATIVE cases that MUST still clone+stay correct: mutate-r-after-bind, return/store/own-arg escape of r, `v.push` after the bind, closure capturing v, shadow-rebind of r/v — run under Linux ASAN+LSan (sanitizers have missed this class before, so build+run E2E + adversarial coverage, not sanitizer-alone). FIXED: added a conservative whitelist borrow-elision pre-pass (src/codegen/borrow_elision.rs, compute_vec_index_borrow_spans, run per fn in compile_function). It marks a `let r = v[i]` RHS span borrow-safe only when, over r's lexical scope (the rest of the enclosing block + nested blocks), every use of r is a recognised read (index-read, .len()/.is_empty(), never a call/own-arg/return/assign/non-read-method/composite-literal) AND v appears only as index-reads (no mutating method, assign, index-store, by-value move) AND there is no closure and no shadow-rebind of r/v; ANY unrecognised construct disqualifies (whitelist-only, so only false-NEGATIVES are possible — never an unsafe borrow). The Let arm (stmts.rs) then skips clone_owned_vec_index_element AND the binding's scope-exit track_vec_* (FreeVecBuffer + recursive drop) for marked spans, so r aliases the element and the container stays the unique owner. Result: the kata-46 read loop drops from 2 mallocs/elem to 1 (matches C); the n!=5040 enumeration's allocation traffic halves. Tests: tests/codegen.rs borrow_elision_elides_read_only_vecvec_index_binding (IR: read-loop clone gone), borrow_elision_keeps_clone_for_unsafe_index_bindings (IR: overwrite/escape/grow/callarg negatives all RETAIN the clone), e2e_borrow_elision_correctness_positive_and_negatives (build+run), and tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_borrow_elision_read_only_vecvec_index_is_clean + asan_borrow_elision_escape_negative_clones_and_is_clean (ASAN: no UAF/double-free; Linux CI LSan covers the leak arm). NOTE: a SEPARATE, pre-existing codegen bug surfaced while testing — `out[j] = nb` index-assignment of a heap Vec element (freeing the old element's buffer) SIGTRAPs (exit 133) on both installed and current karac, independent of this fix; filed/triaged separately (see B-2026-06-19-7).

Index-assignment to a heap-owning element of a nested collection — `out[j] = nb` where `out: Vec[Vec[i64]]` and `nb: Vec[i64]` — SIGTRAPs (exit 133) in the AOT binary on both the installed karac 0.1.0 and current main; `karac run` (interpreter) prints the correct result, so it is a codegen-only fault. The store must drop/free the OLD element's heap buffer before overwriting the {ptr,len,cap} slot; the compiled index-store path appears to mishandle that element drop (double-free / bad-free → SIGTRAP). Discovered while building borrow-elision regression tests for B-2026-06-19-6 (the natural 'overwrite' negative was `let r = out[j]; out[j] = nb; read r`); INDEPENDENT of that fix (the borrow gate correctly clones here, and the crash reproduces with a bare `out[j] = nb` and no `r`). Minimal repro: `fn main() { let mut out: Vec[Vec[i64]] = Vec.new(); let mut b: Vec[i64] = Vec.new(); b.push(5i64); let mut k=0i64; while k<4i64 { out.push(b.clone()); k=k+1i64; } let mut nb: Vec[i64] = Vec.new(); nb.push(99i64); out[0i64] = nb; println(f"{out[0i64][0i64]}") }` → exit 133 built, '99' interpreted. Likely the compile_vec_index_store path for a heap-element (Vec/String/agg) element type not running the prior-element drop, or running it wrongly; compare the push-grow free path and clone_owned_vec_index_element's drop accounting. FIXED: the index-store path now (a) drops the OLD heap element before overwriting and (b) suppresses the moved source binding's scope-exit cleanup. compile_vec_index_store (collections.rs) calls emit_free_vec_buffer_if_owned(elem_ptr) when llvm_ty_is_vec_struct(elem_ty) — freeing the orphaned old buffer (outer-only, matching the Identifier-assign move-overwrite eager free; a live per-element alias keeps its own cleanup, and borrow-elision B-2026-06-19-6 refuses to alias when the container is index-stored in scope, so the freed slot has no borrowed reader). The Index-assign arm in stmts.rs then calls suppress_source_vec_cleanup_for_arg(value) for an OWNING Vec target with a heap-struct element (gated off slice/map element stores), zeroing the moved source's cap so only the container frees the buffer — the same move discipline as Vec.push and the Identifier-assign path. Verified: `out[j]=nb` (single + 10k-loop), fresh call-result RHS, and Vec[String] elements all build+run exit 0 with a flat alloc balance (no per-iteration leak). Tests: tests/codegen.rs e2e_index_store_heap_vec_element_no_double_free + e2e_index_store_heap_string_element_no_double_free (build+run, crash regression); tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_index_store_heap_vec_element_no_double_free (ASAN clean; Linux CI LSan covers the leak arm).

Vec.filled(n, val) with a heap-backed element type bit-copied the SAME fill aggregate into all N slots, so every slot aliased one backing buffer: the canonical 2D DP table `let dp: Vec[Vec[i64]] = Vec.filled(rows, Vec.filled(cols, x))` corrupted across rows on write and N-fold-freed the shared buffer on drop (AOT exit 133 / SIGTRAP). `karac run` (interpreter) was correct (it deep-copies rows), so codegen-only. Surfaced by the cross-kata canonical-idiom audit (DP family: kata #10/#44/#91 edit-distance/wildcard/decode-ways tables). The pre-existing safe case `Vec.filled(n, Vec.new())` works only because the empty `{null,0,0}` aggregate has no buffer to alias. FIXED: build_vec_filled (collections.rs) now deep-clones per slot for non-trivially-copyable element types — it moves `val` into slot 0 and emit_clone_fn_for_type_expr-clones it into slots 1..n (the `vec![x;n]` discipline: N distinct buffers, `val` consumed, no extra drop, no variable-arg double-free). The destination element TypeExpr is threaded from the let binding via a new `pending_let_elem_type_expr` field (the TypeExpr sibling of pending_let_elem_type), CONSUMED at the start of the `filled` arm before the fill argument is compiled so a nested inner Vec.filled(...) does not inherit the outer binding's element type. Trivial element types (i64, flat structs) keep the bit-copy path. Residual: an un-annotated nested-collection fill (no TypeExpr to recover) and 3D+ nesting fall back to bit-copy (no worse than before; 2D + annotated is the canonical case). Tests: tests/codegen.rs e2e_vec_filled_2d_table_rows_independent (build+run row-independence); tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_vec_filled_2d_rows_are_independent_buffers (ASAN clean, 40-byte rows; Linux CI LSan covers the leak arm).

Structural `==`/`!=` on a `shared struct` (design.md § Equality Semantics) was unhandled in both backends despite a correct `#[derive(Eq, PartialEq)]`. INTERPRETER: the `eval_ops.rs` binary-op match had arms for `Value::Struct` and `Value::EnumVariant` but NOT `Value::SharedStruct`, so shared-struct `==` fell through to the `_` arm and produced a runtime error (`operator 'Eq' is not defined for operands of type 'SharedStruct'`); a bare `if a == b` then hit the `Value::Unit not Bool` condition-unreachable panic. FIXED (interpreter): added `(BinOp::Eq|NotEq, SharedStruct, SharedStruct)` arms delegating to `Value`'s PartialEq, which already does full structural comparison (name + immutable_fields + mut_fields + weak-field referent identity, with an Arc::ptr_eq fast path). Verified: separately-built equal-field shared structs compare ==, differing a field compares !=. Test: tests/interpreter.rs test_shared_struct_structural_equality. CODEGEN (follow-on, not yet done): a `shared struct` is a heap RC POINTER, so it misses compile_binary's struct-value path and previously fell through to a confusing `Binary op Eq: left operand has non-comparable type PointerType` error. Replaced that with a clear, actionable diagnostic ('structural == on a shared struct is not yet supported under karac build; works under karac run; field-walk lowering is a tracked follow-on'). The real codegen fix — load and compare each user field through the RC pointer (heap index field+1), recursing for heap fields — mirrors emit_shared_struct_rc_drop_fn's field walk and remains open. NOTE: the audit's report of a *contradictory* 'add #[derive(Eq)]' diagnostic did not reproduce — type_supports_partial_eq (derives.rs) correctly recognizes the derive on shared structs (env_build.rs records derived_traits with is_shared); the typechecker passes the `==`, so the fault was purely the missing backend arms. CODEGEN NOW LANDED (C1, Tier C, 2026-06-20): `emit_shared_struct_eq_fn` (src/codegen/synth.rs) synthesizes `bool(ptr,ptr)` over the two RC pointers — an `Arc::ptr_eq` identity fast-path (ptrtoint+icmp) then a field-by-field walk through the heap layout via `shared_gep_layout` (skips the RC header), registered BEFORE recursing so a self-referential shared struct doesn't loop the emitter; nested shared-struct fields load the inner RC pointer and recurse, every other field kind goes through the existing slot-based `emit_eq_fn_for_type_expr`. Wired at the operator site in compile_expr's Binary arm (src/codegen/exprs.rs), mirroring the heap-payload-enum special-case: recover the struct name via `shared_type_for_expr`, guard `!is_enum` + both-pointer operands, negate for `!=`. The residual RC-pointer honest-Err in compile_binop_typed now fires only for a `shared enum` or a `shared struct` nested inside a by-value struct (no per-field type name at the compile_struct_eq recursion site) — both noted follow-ons. A/B verified (direct scalar ==, self-compare, nested shared-struct fields, String field, !=); ASAN-clean. Tests: tests/codegen.rs test_e2e_shared_struct_structural_equality; tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_shared_struct_structural_eq_no_leak_no_double_free.

`{checked,saturating,overflowing}_{add,sub,mul}` — documented integer methods (design.md § Arithmetic Overflow, the table at ~2146: checked_*→Option[Self], saturating_*→Self, overflowing_*→(Self,bool)) — were entirely unrecognized: `no method 'checked_add' on type 'i32'`, and a bare match on the (never-produced) result hit a secondary interpreter exhaustiveness panic. Surfaced by the cross-kata audit (math-bit family). Unlike `wrapping_*` (typechecker-restricted to the 64-bit widths, narrow-width deferred), these are defined on EVERY integer width — the audit's repro is i32. FIXED (typechecker + interpreter): typechecker (expr_method_call.rs) now accepts the 9 methods on any Int/UInt receiver with the right return shapes (literal-promotion + same-type arg rule mirrored from wrapping_*); interpreter (method_call.rs) implements them width-correctly. Width recovery: read the ARGUMENT's recorded type from expr_types (the same span→type table narrow_oob uses) — NOT the receiver's, because a MethodCall's Expr.span aliases its receiver span, so a chained `x.checked_mul(y).is_none()` would overwrite the receiver-span type with the outer call's `bool`; the argument is a distinct leaf the typechecker pins to the receiver type. Signed widths (i8/i16/i32/i64) and narrow unsigned (u8/u16/u32) compute exactly in i128 with [lo,hi] bounds; 64-bit unsigned (u64/usize) reinterprets the Value::Int(i64) two's-complement bits as u64 (full-range correct). Verified across i32 (checked None on overflow / saturating MAX / overflowing wraps+flags), i64, u8 (sub underflow→0), u32 (mul overflow, saturating→u32::MAX). Test: tests/interpreter.rs test_overflow_arith_methods_width_correct. CODEGEN (follow-on, not yet done): the width-specific lowering (llvm.{s,u}{add,sub,mul}.with.overflow + .sat intrinsics, building Option[Self] via build_option_some_via_phis / the (Self,bool) tuple / the clamped Self) is a tracked follow-on; codegen currently emits a clear 'not yet supported under karac build; works under karac run' diagnostic instead of falling through to method-not-found. CODEGEN NOW LANDED (C2, Tier C, 2026-06-20): lowered at the receiver's declared width via `llvm.{s,u}{op}.with.overflow.iN` (`emit_overflow_intrinsic`, the non-trapping sibling of `emit_checked_int_arith`, src/codegen/expr_ops.rs). One `(wrapped, did_overflow)` pair feeds all three families (src/codegen/method_call.rs): checked_* -> Option[T] via `build_option_some_via_phis` (payload coerced to the i64 Option slot, zext/sext); overflowing_* -> a `{iN,i1}` tuple (narrow T keeps its real width, matching `llvm_type_for_type_expr`); saturating_* -> `select(ovf, bound, wrapped)` where the bound is UMAX/0 (unsigned add-mul/sub) or, signed, SMAX/SMIN by the true-result sign (add/sub `a>=0?SMAX:SMIN`, mul `sign(a)==sign(b)?SMAX:SMIN`) — bound-select used uniformly because `llvm.*mul.sat` doesn't exist and widen-to-i128 breaks on u64 mul. Operands truncated from i64 value-flow back to iN first (`coerce_int_to`); width+signedness via `receiver_int_kind`. A/B verified against the interpreter test's exact program + a signed-saturating sign sweep. Tests: tests/codegen.rs test_e2e_overflow_arith_family, test_e2e_overflow_arith_signed_saturating_signs.

A heap value (Vec[T] / String) captured READ-ONLY by MULTIPLE sibling TaskGroup.spawn tasks while the parent still owns it — the canonical parallel-stencil fan-out `for band: tg.spawn(|| f(grid, lo, hi))` where the closures pass the shared buffer to a `ref` param — was miscompiled: the blocking-spawn lowering in codegen/task_group.rs treated EVERY capture as a by-move transfer, so each task re-registered a free of the captured {data,len,cap} buffer (lower_spawn_shared's FreeVecBuffer re-registration) AND the parent's scope-exit free was suppressed. With N tasks capturing the one shared buffer, it was freed N times — a double-free / use-after-free. Symptoms: wrong results (a 4-task band-sum of 0..99 returned 328/332 instead of 4950, non-deterministic) and an allocator abort 'failed to lock mutex: Invalid argument (os error 22)'. Reading only the copied scalar header field (data.len()) was fine; only dereferencing the shared buffer corrupted. The cross-task-safe check (cross_task_safe.rs) did not catch it — plain Vec/String are not on the five-leaf unsafe list and the body's *type* is safe; the flaw was the lowering's ownership assumption, not the capture type. Moving a unique buffer into one task (each task owns its own) always worked. ROOT: spawn lowering ignored the ownership pass's per-capture mode (closure_capture_paths / OwnershipMode), which par {} branches already honor (ParCaptureMode::Copy: a shared Vec read across branches is passed by value, parent frees once after the join barrier). FIX: lower_spawn_shared now classifies each captured root as borrow (every path Ref/MutRef) vs move (any Own); a borrowed capture on the blocking path neither re-registers the wrapper-side free nor suppresses the parent's free, so the parent frees the buffer exactly once after the structured-concurrency join barrier — the design.md Structured Concurrency Lifetime Guarantees borrow ('a parallel task may legally borrow a binding ... the task joins strictly before the cleanup fires'). Moves and the coroutine-spawn path keep the by-value transfer. This also resolves the latent leak the suppress-only B-2026-06-18-8 fix left for a single borrowed capture (parent now frees it). Tests: tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_taskgroup_spawn_shared_vec_read_across_tasks_single_free + asan_taskgroup_spawn_shared_string_read_across_tasks_single_free (value correctness 4950/hits-3 + ASAN no-double-free; Linux LSan covers the leak arm — verified green via scripts/lsan-local.sh taskgroup_spawn, all 6 incl. the existing move/loop-borrow cases).

Two width-dependent integer scalar method families were unrecognized (`no method 'pow'/'count_ones' on type ...`), surfaced by the cross-kata math-bit audit (Tier-A A1/A3). FIXED complete across all three backends. (1) `n.pow(k) -> Self`: repeated-multiply power, exponent is `u32` (Rust's `iN::pow(self, exp: u32)`; suffix-free literal promotes, otherwise must be u32 with an `as u32` hint), traps `integer overflow` at the receiver width exactly as the `*` it iterates. Interpreter (method_call.rs eval_int_pow) uses square-and-multiply with i128 range checks (the squared base can't false-trap because its exponent 2^k <= exp); codegen (codegen/method_call.rs) emits a counted loop whose body reuses emit_checked_int_arith("mul",..) so an out-of-range partial product traps at the declared width. (2) `count_ones`/`leading_zeros`/`trailing_zeros` -> u32: width-correct (count within the receiver's declared bits; a signed iN is masked to width first, so i8 -1 has 8 set bits and u8 0 has 8 leading/trailing zeros, NOT the i64-widened 64). Codegen lowers to llvm.ctpop/ctlz/cttz on the narrowed iN (ctlz/cttz with is_zero_poison=false → bit-width on zero, matching Rust/interp). WIDTH RECOVERY: both families need the declared receiver width, but a MethodCall's Expr.span aliases its receiver span and the generic infer_expr post-record clobbers expr_types[receiver.span] with the call's result type (Self for pow — harmless — but u32 for the bit intrinsics). Fix: thread the non-aliased args_close_span (the `)` leaf) into infer_method_call + eval_method_call; the typechecker stashes the receiver type there and the interpreter reads it back clobber-free. Codegen (which has no general expr_types and gets only call_span) recovers width from the existing method_callee_types["<recv>.<method>"] record with a clobber-detection fallback to the receiver's declared type / literal suffix, then operates at that width and re-extends the result to the i64-backed representation narrow ints flow in. Tests: tests/interpreter.rs test_int_pow_values_and_zero_exponent / test_int_pow_overflow_traps_at_receiver_width / test_bit_intrinsics_width_correct; tests/codegen.rs e2e_int_pow_values / e2e_int_pow_overflow_traps / e2e_bit_intrinsics_width_correct (A/B parity with interp); tests/typechecker.rs int_pow_ok_on_every_width_with_u32_exponent / int_pow_rejects_non_u32_exponent / bit_intrinsics_return_u32_on_every_width.

`char.to_digit(radix) -> Option[u32]` (Rust's char::to_digit) was unrecognized (Tier-A A2). FIXED typechecker + interpreter: typechecker (expr_method_call.rs) accepts it on a char receiver, radix is u32 (suffix-free literal promotes, else `as u32` hint), result Option[u32]; interpreter (method_call.rs) delegates to Rust char::to_digit, returning Some(value)/None, and traps an out-of-range radix (< 2 or > 36) matching Rust's panic. CODEGEN (follow-on, not done): emits an honest 'not yet supported under karac build; works under karac run' diagnostic (the Option[u32] construction lowering is shared with the checked_to_* float->int follow-on), gated on a char receiver so a user `to_digit` is unaffected. BONUS BUG (latent ICE this surfaced): a faulting expression in `match` SCRUTINEE position (index OOB, div-by-zero, or an in-scrutinee trap like to_digit(bad_radix)) sets pending_cf and yields a poison value; the match handler (eval_expr.rs) ran eval_match on it and hit a non-exhaustive `unreachable!` when the poison matched no arm (a wildcard arm masked this before; Some/None arms exposed it). FIXED: the Match arm now short-circuits on pending_cf before eval_match, mirroring the Binary/Unary operand-fault short-circuits. Tests: tests/interpreter.rs test_char_to_digit_some_none_and_radix / test_char_to_digit_radix_out_of_range_traps / test_faulting_match_scrutinee_propagates_not_ice; tests/typechecker.rs char_to_digit_returns_option_u32 / char_to_digit_rejects_non_u32_radix; tests/codegen.rs char_to_digit_codegen_emits_honest_error. CODEGEN FOLLOW-ON DONE (4e4b57de), closing the partial: char.to_digit(radix) now LOWERS under karac build (was an honest 'not yet supported' diagnostic). method_call.rs: radix ∉ 2..=36 traps (emit_panic, div-guard trap-BB pattern) matching Rust/interp; digit value by ASCII class ('0'..'9'→c-'0'; 'a'..'z'→c-'a'+10; 'A'..'Z'→c-'A'+10; else no digit) selected branch-free; valid iff a class matched AND value<radix (unsigned) → Some(v)/None via the shared build_checked_to_int_option Option constructor (made pub(super)). Gated on a char receiver. A/B-verified interp==seq build==auto-par build (values + radix trap). Tests: codegen e2e_char_to_digit_codegen (replaced obsolete char_to_digit_codegen_emits_honest_error), par_codegen test_e2e_auto_par_char_to_digit. Green: codegen 2007, par_codegen 150, interpreter char to_digit; fmt+clippy(llvm) clean.

fix: By-value SoA params DONE in 74bbbef7 (slice 1): a bare Vec[E] param whose name matches a layout block now lowers to the 4-field SoA struct in declare_function (via soa_value_param_layout) with a compile_function prologue that spills the moved-in struct to a directly-GEP'd slot; caller-retains ownership (no callee free, no double-free), no call-site marshalling. Tests: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_soa_by_value_param_read_across_function + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_soa_by_value_param_caller_retains_no_leak_or_double_free (LSan-clean). Forward arg-layout mono DONE in 9beaa258 (slice 2): a by-value SoA Vec[E] crosses a call boundary regardless of param name, routed to an on-demand layout monomorph keyed on the caller's argument layout (fn_asts + ensure_layout_mono_generated). SoA RETURN values DONE in 72298356 (slice 3): backward inference keys the return layout off the receiving `let recv = f()` binding; declare_mono_function lowers the return type to the SoA struct and compile_mono_function seeds the returned local(s) SoA, with suppress_soa_cleanup_for_tail_identifier moving the buffers out to the caller (no double-free/leak). Tests: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_soa_return_value_caller_different_name + ::test_e2e_soa_return_two_layouts_through_one_builder_distinct_monos + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_soa_return_value_caller_owns_no_leak_or_double_free. Multi-buffer / differing-name (BORROW forms) DONE in 6aa7afd9/238e1388 (slice 4): forward layout-flow inference extended to ref Vec[E] / mut ref Vec[E] params (param_is_layout_carrying peels one ref/mut-ref), so multiple SoA buffers of one element type flow through shared by-ref helpers regardless of param name, each a distinct monomorph (total$data_soa_grid vs total$data_soa_coll). A borrow param keeps its pointer ABI (active_param_soa_layout guarded to by-value only); compile_mono_function's prologue registers a SoA borrow param in ref_params so the access paths deref the slot once (without it: pointer bytes read as the SoA struct -> garbage len -> SIGTRAP); ref_params is now save/restored around both mono entry points. Tests: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_soa_by_ref_param_caller_different_name + ::test_e2e_soa_two_buffers_through_one_ref_helper_distinct_monos + ::test_e2e_soa_mut_ref_push_across_function + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_soa_mut_ref_fill_borrow_no_leak_or_double_free (Linux-LSan-clean). Origin-only soa_layouts cutover DONE in db584a09/8ad88b24 (slice 5): the access-path trigger now reads a per-binding LayoutId value carrier (binding_layouts, seeded at the binding site by seed_binding_site_layout) instead of re-deriving SoA-ness from the binding name against soa_layouts at each use; active_layout_id consults layout_subst then binding_layouts with no soa_layouts fallback, so soa_layouts is origin-only (catalogue + LayoutId->shape + binding-site name-match). The redundant name-keyed by-value param ABI (soa_value_param_layout) is retired: the base symbol lowers every Vec[E] param AoS and a SoA arg routes to a mono regardless of param name, fixing the footgun where a base param merely sharing a name with a layout block lowered SoA on the name alone (an AoS arg then marshalled a 3-field struct into a 4-field slot). Cross-group disjointness audited: the borrow checker is layout-agnostic (codegen containment), groups partition fields, so cross-group borrows are already-disjoint field places -- no new fact needed. Tests: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_soa_layout_named_param_base_is_aos_mono_is_soa + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_soa_layout_named_param_base_aos_and_mono_soa_no_leak. Slice 6 (Slipstream full-SoA PROOF) DONE in b9138d30: examples/slipstream/src/sim.kara's carried LBM grid (plus per-substep coll/next) is a layout block split into two cache groups; the per-band chunks stay AoS (they cross the generic TaskHandle[Vec[LbmNode]] join). The native oracle's milestone framebuffer checksums are byte-identical AoS<->SoA (1582897806 / 793640938 / 680974524) and the browser flagship runs on SoA in real headless Chrome (verify_browser.mjs PASS). The proof surfaced and fixed FIVE more cross-function gaps (all in the compiler, no demo-side workarounds): (1) with_capacity SoA constructor -- the presize pass rewrites a counted-loop-filled Vec.new() into Vec.with_capacity(n) (init_grid/fan_collide shape) and the SoA let path matched only Vec.new, so the rewritten binding kept the AoS slot under an SoA layout; is_vec_with_capacity_call routes it to compile_soa_new. (2) Returned-local base-symbol clash -- a builder whose returned local is named after a layout block (init_grid's grid) name-matched its body SoA while the AoS-return base symbol returned the 3-field Vec; soa_return_locals suppresses the origin name-match for a returned local (its layout is the return mono's). (3) SoA reassignment grid = substep(grid,..) -- the carried-grid per-frame double-buffer had no backward-mono path on the assignment arm (only the let arm); compile_soa_assign_from_call parks the return layout, frees the OLD groups (by-value param is caller-retains), stores the new SoA header (Linux-LSan clean, no double-free/leak). (4) Tail-CALL SoA-return propagation -- a SoA-returning fn whose body ENDS IN a layout-returning call (substep's fan_stream(coll,..)) returned AoS while its signature was SoA; compile_tail_final_expr flows the return layout to the tail call. (5) SoA across a coroutine suspend (browser render loop's grid carried across frames.recv()) -- collect soa_layouts + pre-populate fn_asts BEFORE the state-machine emission (the poll-fn body's SoA-return inference reads both), size an SoA persisted local's state-struct field as the 4-field struct, and type an SoA binding's par-block/auto-par return slot SoA (infer_let_binding_llvm_type). Tests: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_soa_{counted_loop_fill_returned,returned_local_name_matches_layout,reassign_carried_buffer,tail_call_soa_return} + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_soa_reassign_carried_buffer_no_leak_or_double_free. Gates: codegen E2E 1696/0, par_codegen 34/0, coro_e2e 122/0, Linux-LSan soa 12/0. FOLLOW-ONS (separate features, never miscompile -- degrade to AoS or unbuilt): branch-leaf/multi-return SoA returns degrade to AoS (spike §8); the whole-element SoA index-store grid[i] = E{..} is unbuilt even single-function (push + mut-ref field write-back + field-level vec[i].field= via B-2026-06-20-7 all work; the whole-element scatter is the gap). The field-level SoA index-store blocker is FIXED (B-2026-06-20-7). See docs/spikes/per-layout-monomorphization.md. FOLLOW-ONS (2026-06-20, commits c74752f6 + 3ccab359): (1) whole-element SoA index-store grid[i]=E{...} — compile_soa_index_store scatters the RHS element's fields into each group buffer at [i] (the push-at-len decomposition, bounds-checked, no growth; deref a ref/mut-ref param slot for cross-function scatter); pre-fix it fell into compile_vec_index_store and wrote whole AoS elements over one group's narrower stride (silent heap-buffer-overflow). 3 codegen E2E (single-fn, mut-ref cross-fn, cold-group) + 1 ASAN overflow guard. POD elements only (the whole SoA subsystem assumes POD; heap-field elements stay an orthogonal gap). (2) branch-leaf / multi-return SoA returns — soa_return_local_names now recursively collects EVERY bare-identifier return site (each explicit return <id>; in any branch/loop/nested block except closures, plus every tail leaf of a branch-bearing tail if c {a} else {b}), so each return value lowers SoA against the patched signature; pre-fix multi-site returns were a hard LLVM 'return type does not match' verify failure (NOT the silent AoS degrade the spike assumed). Early-return move-out uses a branch-safe runtime cap=0 sentinel (neutralize_moved_soa_groups_slot), not the tail path's compile-time FreeSoaGroups frame removal — the early-return frame is shared with the fall-through path where the local is not returned and must still be freed (branch-buried-move footgun). 3 codegen E2E + 2 ASAN/LSan (early-return fall-through, branch-leaf tails — both paths). Native Slipstream oracle byte-identical post-change (1582897806/793640938/680974524); full codegen 1713, par 34, coro 122, SoA LSan 15 all green.
title: SoA `layout` blocks did not cross function boundaries — passing a SoA-laid-out Vec[E] to another function miscompiled. The layout makes the binding-site a 4-field SoA value {g0_ptr,...,len,cap} (soa_layouts is a name-keyed codegen map), but a callee's `Vec[E]` parameter is always compiled with the default AoS {ptr,len,cap} representation, and the SoA access paths GEP the param's slot alloca directly. By-VALUE: caller loads the 4-field SoA and passes it to a 3-field AoS param -> LLVM 'Call parameter type does not match function signature' verification failure (loud). By-REF (`ref Vec[E]`): the slot alloca holds a POINTER to the caller's SoA struct, but compile_soa_index_read / compile_soa_method GEP'd the alloca as if it were the struct -> reads the pointer's bytes as group-ptrs/len -> garbage len (e.g. 1073741824) -> SIGTRAP (exit 133). A SILENT miscompile. AoS (no layout block) is correct; single-function SoA (push/index/field/len all within the declaring fn) is correct. Surfaced by the Slipstream LBM dogfood (examples/slipstream): its kernel splits the grid across `ref Vec[LbmNode]` helpers (collide_band/stream_band/velocities/render_fb), so it could not use SoA at all -- exactly the 'SoA layout' capability the roster bills it on. PARTIAL FIX: the by-REF READ path now works. compile_soa_index_read (collections.rs) and compile_soa_method (exprs.rs) compute a `soa_struct_ptr` that derefs the slot alloca once when the binding is a `ref`/`mut ref` param (self.ref_params), so the callee operates on the caller's existing SoA struct; a by-value/`let` binding's slot already IS the struct (no deref). Now working cross-function: .len(), whole-element index read (`let e = es[i]`), and direct indexed field access (`es[i].y`, which routes through compile_soa_index_read). Regression: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_soa_by_ref_param_read_across_function (sum 21 via a `ref Vec[E]` helper; was SIGTRAP). All 1670 codegen tests pass, fmt+clippy clean. REMAINING (ABI-level follow-on, not yet done): by-VALUE SoA params (callee signature must be the SoA struct type + call must marshal the SoA value + param prologue) and SoA RETURN values; plus the name-keyed model fits multi-buffer kernels (grid/coll/next) awkwardly -- the fuller design is per-layout monomorphization (design.md Feature 1, P1.5/Phase 11).

Passing a bare named `fn` as a first-class `Fn(...)` value miscompiles. `fn apply(f: Fn(i64)->i64, x: i64) -> i64 { f(x) }` called as `apply(doubler, 21)` (doubler a named fn) parses + type-checks fine, but codegen lowers `TypeKind::FnType` to the default `i64` (types_lowering.rs llvm_type_for_type_expr falls through) while a bare fn name lowers to a raw `ptr` (exprs.rs free-fn-as-value), so the higher-order call fails LLVM module verification: 'Call parameter type does not match function signature! ptr @doubler ... call i64 @apply(ptr @doubler, i64 21)'. Only the special-cased `Server.serve(handler)` dispatch works today (landed 2026-05-09, commit 60ce3a4); the general `Fn`-typed-parameter path is unsupported and was untracked. Workaround: wrap in a closure (`apply(|x| doubler(x), 21)`), which builds the {fn_ptr,env} fat pointer. Fix = lower FnType to the closure fat-pointer struct {ptr,ptr} and reify a bare fn name into {ptr, null-env} at Fn-typed arg sites (or have the Fn ABI accept a raw ptr). Surfaced writing the `#[inline(always)]`-through-fn-value IR test for the codegen-hint-attrs slice (phase-8-stdlib-floor.md line 775); the attribute itself emits correctly regardless of call shape, so only the indirect-call test is blocked. FIXED 2026-06-21 (79f1de14): lower `FnType` to the `{fn_ptr,env_ptr}` closure fat pointer (types_lowering.rs FnType arm — was the 8-byte i64 fall-through); register a `Fn`-typed parameter in closure_fn_types via the env-first closure_abi_fn_type so a body call `f(x)` routes through compile_closure_call as an indirect call (functions.rs); and reify a bare fn name at a `Fn`-typed arg site into `{trampoline, null env}` through a memoized, env-ignoring forwarder `__karac_fnval_<name>` (reify_named_fn_as_fn_value, call_dispatch.rs) so a plain free fn (no env param) conforms to the env-first ABI. Trampoline signature and the param's indirect-call type are both built from the same annotation, so they agree. A closure LITERAL into a `Fn(...)` param now works too (already a fat pointer; no reify). Tests: tests/codegen.rs fn_value_named_fn_as_fn_typed_param_runs / fn_value_fn_typed_param_multiarg_and_float_run / fn_value_named_fn_reused_across_call_sites (one memoized trampoline) / fn_value_closure_literal_into_fn_typed_param_run / test_ir_fn_value_named_fn_reified_trampoline; leak gate tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_named_fn_value_heap_arg_no_leak (heap String arg through the trampoline freed once — green on the Linux ASAN+LSan harness).

Four allocating String->String methods were unrecognized (Tier-B B1): `trim()` / `to_lowercase()` / `to_uppercase()` (no-arg -> String) and `replace(from, to)` (two String args -> String). FIXED complete across all three backends with FULL UNICODE semantics matching Rust's str stdlib exactly — `to_lowercase`/`to_uppercase` are Unicode case maps that can CHANGE byte length (`straße`.to_uppercase() == `STRASSE`), `trim` strips Unicode whitespace, `replace` is byte-substring. Typechecker (stdlib_seq.rs infer_str_method): arg-shape validation + String result. Interpreter (method_call_seq.rs try_eval_seq_method): direct Rust `s.trim().to_string()` / `s.to_lowercase()` / `s.to_uppercase()` / `s.replace(from,to)`. NOTE the interpreter `replace` arm is String-guarded and the vector handler (try_eval_vector_method, SIMD `Vector.replace`) runs BEFORE the seq handler, so a Vector receiver never reaches the new String arm. CODEGEN: new runtime helpers in runtime/src/clone.rs — `karac_string_{to_lowercase,to_uppercase,trim}(data, len, *mut out_len) -> ptr` and `karac_string_replace(data, len, from, from_len, to, to_len, *mut out_len) -> ptr`, each computing the result via the SAME Rust stdlib call as the interpreter (so the two backends are bit-identical on Unicode without shipping Unicode tables into codegen) and returning a fresh NUL-terminated `{ptr, out_len, out_len}` (cap==len) buffer (null+0 for empty, the karac_string_slice convention). codegen.rs declares the 4 externs; vec_method.rs adds the dispatch arms (load receiver {data,len}, extract arg {ptr,len} pairs, call helper with an out_len alloca, build the String aggregate via the shared build_string_xform_result helper). The result is freed exactly once by the existing String scope-cleanup registration (same path as repeat/slice). Decision: full Unicode (not an ASCII-only shortcut) — an ASCII `to_lowercase` would be a silent correctness footgun on non-ASCII AND would diverge from the interpreter's Rust-Unicode result. trim allocates an OWNED copy rather than returning a borrowed view (a String-view story is a future optimization). Tests: tests/interpreter.rs test_string_trim_replace_case_interpreter; tests/codegen.rs e2e_string_trim_replace_case_codegen (A/B parity incl. ß->SS and Unicode é); tests/typechecker.rs string_trim_replace_case_return_string / string_trim_rejects_arguments / string_replace_rejects_non_string_arg; tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_string_trim_replace_case_no_leak_no_double_free (macOS ASAN double-free/UAF + Linux LSan leak gate via scripts/lsan-local.sh, 36-byte payload, 1000x loop -> 143000).

`Vec.binary_search(x)` / `Slice.binary_search(x) -> Option[i64]` were typecheck- and interpreter-complete but had NO codegen — `karac build` failed loudly ('Vec/String method binary_search is not yet supported in codegen' / 'no handler for slice method binary_search'). Tier-B B2. FIXED in codegen for integer (any width, signed + unsigned) and String element types; float/struct/other element types emit an honest 'not yet supported under karac build; works under karac run' diagnostic (the interpreter handles them via value_compare). The search REPLICATES Rust's CURRENT branchless `slice::binary_search_by` EXACTLY (narrow-to-base: size=len,base=0; while size>1 { half=size/2; mid=base+half; base = cmp(v[mid],x)>0 ? base : mid; size-=half } then cmp(v[base],x)==0 ? Some(base):None) — NOT the textbook return-on-first-equal variant, which picks a DIFFERENT index among DUPLICATE keys; since the interpreter calls std's binary_search_by, codegen had to match it bit-for-bit (verified: [1,2,2,2,2,3,4].binary_search(2) == 4, [5,5,5,5].binary_search(5) == 3 on both backends). The 3-way element compare (emit_binary_search_cmp, src/codegen/vec_method.rs) is pure data-flow (no basic blocks): integers widen to i64 (zext for uN, sext for iN — matching the interpreter's signed i64 value_compare where uint values are non-negative i64) then signed 3-way via selects; String elements route through the existing karac_string_cmp runtime fn (byte-lexicographic, == Rust String Ord, no archive rebuild). Shared compile_binary_search helper drives BOTH the Vec arm (3-field {ptr,len,cap} header, compile_vec_method) and the Slice arm (2-field {ptr,len} header, the slice dispatch in method_call.rs); element type name recovered via var_elem_type_exprs (vec_elem_type_name). A fresh-owned String needle temp is freed once (free_fresh_owned_str_arg); the receiver's elements are read-only (never freed). Tests: tests/codegen.rs e2e_vec_binary_search_codegen (A/B parity: Vec i64/u8/String + Slice + empty + duplicates); tests/interpreter.rs test_vec_binary_search_duplicates_and_widths (pins std's duplicate-index reference); tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_vec_binary_search_string_needle_no_leak_no_double_free (fresh-needle free path, macOS ASAN + Linux LSan, 36-byte payloads).

String `==`/`!=` codegen memcmp'd `l_len` bytes from BOTH operand pointers UNCONDITIONALLY (compile_string_binop, BinOp::Eq|NotEq in src/codegen/expr_ops.rs), so when the left operand is LONGER than the right it read past the end of the shorter RIGHT buffer — a heap-buffer-overflow (ASAN-caught; a latent out-of-bounds read in release, mostly masked because libc memcmp short-circuits at the first differing byte, so it only actually overruns when the operands share a prefix at least as long as the shorter buffer, e.g. "OnceFnHandler" vs "OnceFn"). The Lt/Gt arm already clamped to min_len; only the equality arm used raw l_len. Surfaced during phase-12 self-hosting slice 3a: the parser's `current_ident_matches` borrow-matches a String payload out of an indexed place (`self.tokens[self.pos].token`) and compares it against a short keyword (`dup_str(n) == "Fn"` / `"OnceFn"`); a type name longer than the keyword overran the keyword literal's buffer. ORIGINALLY MIS-ATTRIBUTED to a double-free of the borrowed enum payload (the slice-3a writeup's 'latent codegen double-free on a repeated indexed-enum-payload borrow-match'); investigation showed the borrow-match is SOUND — the indexed enum scrutinee is deep-cloned by clone_borrowed_index_field_enum_scrutinee (#38), so the bound payload owns an independent buffer freed exactly once, and equal-length comparisons of any peek/move count are ASAN-clean. The real, reproducible defect is this `==` overread; the real multi-file buggy parser was ASAN-dirty before and is clean after, with NO second error behind the overread. FIX: clamp the equality memcmp span to min(l_len, r_len) (mirrors the Lt/Gt arm). Memory-safe and semantics-preserving — the existing `len_eq && data_eq` AND already forces unequal-length operands to compare false, and when lengths match min_len == l_len == r_len (identical to the prior code). Regression: tests/memory_sanitizer.rs asan_string_eq_mismatched_len_no_overread_in_indexed_payload_match — a multi-variant enum whose String payload is borrow-matched out of a struct-owned Vec element and compared against shorter, prefix-sharing literals; verified it fails (heap-buffer-overflow READ) on the pre-fix l_len memcmp and passes after. Green: memory_sanitizer 299, codegen 1684, selfhost_parser + selfhost_lexer.

No ordered key->value map existed (Tier-B B3) — only `SortedSet` (ordered set) and `Map` (insertion-order hash map). Added `SortedMap[K: Ord, V]`, the B-tree-backed key->value sibling of `SortedSet`, full across typecheck + interpreter; codegen is interpreter-only + honest Err (same v1 status as SortedSet, which also has no B-tree runtime). NEW Value::SortedMap(BTreeMap<OrdValue, Value>) keyed by the existing OrdValue (value_compare ordering, == SortedSet). Registration: prelude.rs PRELUDE_TYPES + STDLIB_SOURCES bake of runtime/stdlib/sorted_map.kara (struct SortedMap[=K,=V]{}) + stub_generics 2-param; resolver scope-0 picks it up via PRELUDE_TYPES. Methods (typechecker stdlib_map.rs infer_sorted_map_method, K:Ord bound via type_supports_ord; interpreter across method_call_seq/map/set): core map surface len/is_empty/contains_key/get->Option[V]/get_or->V/insert->Option[V](old)/remove->Option[V]/keys->Vec[K]/values->Vec[V]/entries->Vec[(K,V)]/merge(last-writer-wins)/clear, PLUS the ordered queries min/max->Option[(K,V)], floor(k)/ceiling(k)->Option[(K,V)] (largest key<=k / smallest key>=k via BTreeMap::range cursor), range(lo,hi)->Vec[(K,V)] (INCLUSIVE [lo,hi], inverted bounds yield empty). keys/values/entries and `for pair in m` iterate ascending key order. Dispatch wired through the existing Map/Set/SortedSet handler chain (set handler: clear/min/max/range/floor/ceiling on &obj; map handler: get_or/keys/values/entries/merge/insert/remove on owned clone; seq handler: len/is_empty/get/contains_key/clone). Support sites mirrored from SortedSet/Map: value.rs (PartialEq/Display/debug_fmt/variant_name), helpers.rs (value_compare lexicographic over (k,v) pairs + discriminant 14), exec.rs deep_clone_value explicit arm (collection VALUES get fresh storage, not aliased Arc), eval_expr/method_call_iter (for-loop + .iter() yield (k,v) tuples), env_build Item=(K,V), derives Clone/Display/Debug, types iterator_item/clone_self, effectchecker+ownership (insert/remove/clear/merge=MutRef seed allocates(Heap); reads=ImmRef), alloc_rejection, fallible_alloc. CODEGEN (interpreter-only): SortedMap.new() is intercepted in call_dispatch.rs compile_call with an honest 'SortedMap is interpreter-only in v1 ... Run under karac run, or use Map[K,V]' Err (every SortedMap program must construct before use, so the constructor gate gives ONE clean message instead of a downstream 'no handler for method' codegen-bug fall-through that SortedSet still emits); types_lowering/declarations/clone_drop/entry_chains/param_own add SortedMap alongside SortedSet so param/return lowering + try_clone never ICE. NO new runtime symbol, NO archive rebuild (BTreeMap lives in the interpreter only). Decision: full ordered API incl. floor/ceiling/range (user-requested) over a min/max-only minimal set. No memory_sanitizer test — interpreter-only means no AOT binary to ASAN. Tests: tests/interpreter.rs 12 (test_sorted_map_*: new/len, insert/get/contains, remove, ordered iteration, min/max + empty, floor/ceiling, range incl. inverted, get_or, string keys sorted, clone independence + clear, merge last-writer-wins); tests/typechecker.rs 16 (return types of every method, SortedMap[K,V] annotation, K:Ord bound rejection on a non-derived distinct type, unknown-method NoMethodFound); tests/codegen.rs test_sorted_map_codegen_rejected_interpreter_only (build fails loud with the interpreter-only message).

fix: FIXED in 38fb0b57 via a new compile_soa_field_store (collections.rs): locate the field's owning group + its within-group position, then address group_buf[index] by THAT group's sub-struct stride and GEP the field slot (the store-side mirror of compile_soa_index_read's group addressing — same ref-param deref, same bounds check, same coerce_to_struct_field_ty). compile_field_store (expr_ops.rs) routes an indexed SoA object here FIRST (gated on active_soa_layout), ahead of the indexed-shared / nested-plain-struct branches that caused the bug. Compound assignment `bodies[i].field += expr` composes for free (read via compile_soa_index_read, store via the new path). Tests: tests/codegen.rs::test_e2e_soa_field_index_store_strided (cross-group write+read, 164 — a dropped index-1 store gives 161.5) + tests/memory_sanitizer.rs::asan_soa_field_index_store_no_overflow (field scatter across both groups, 20x loop — the old OOB store trips ASAN). ROOT CAUSE: compile_field_store had no SoA branch, so `bodies[i].x = expr` fell into the nested-plain-struct path which treats the SoA struct as a contiguous AoS element — it read the SoA header's field-0 pointer as the data base and strided by the FULL element size. Index 0 coincidentally hit group-0 slot 0; index >= 1 wrote PAST the group buffer (a silent heap overflow), so the store was dropped. Repro (single function, no call boundary): struct Body { x: f64, vx: f64, health: f64 }; layout bodies: Vec[Body] { group pos { x } group vel { vx } group hp { health } }; push two elements, then `bodies[0].x = 7.0; bodies[1].x = 9.0; println(bodies[0].x + bodies[1].x)`. Codegen (SoA) prints 17; the AoS interpreter oracle prints 16. The field-level SoA index-store writes element index 0 correctly but the store at index 1 lands at neither slot 1 nor slot 0 (read-back of bodies[0].x is still 7, bodies[1].x is still its original 10) -- the per-group destination address is computed wrong for index >= 1 (likely a missing index*elem_stride term / wrong group base, mirroring nothing like compile_soa_index_read's correct read addressing), so non-zero-index field scatters are effectively dropped. Pure cross-group READS (bodies[i].x + bodies[i].health) are correct (covered by existing tests). PRE-EXISTING: the base compiler at 6296b9ae miscompiles it byte-identically, so it is NOT introduced by per-layout-mono slice 5 (db584a09, which only swapped the layout value carrier). Independent of the borrow checker (a codegen address-arithmetic fault, not an aliasing-fact gap; surfaced while auditing cross-group disjointness for the e.position += e.velocity idiom). Same family as the unbuilt whole-element index-store grid[i] = E{..} (still open). UNBLOCKS B-2026-06-19-14 slice 6 (Slipstream's LBM kernel scatters field updates by index).
title: Field-level SoA index-store `vec[i].field = expr` is dropped for index >= 1 -- the per-group destination address is not strided by the element index, so only element 0 stores correctly (codegen 17 vs AoS-oracle 16 on a 2-element repro). Single-function; no call boundary involved. Surfaced during the per-layout-mono slice-5 cross-group disjointness audit while probing the field-scatter write idiom.

Tier-D D1: `Map.entry(k).or_insert(d)` write-through (the `mut ref V` contract, design.md § Entry[K,V]) was broken in BOTH backends — the flagship counter `*m.entry(k).or_insert(0) += 1` produced WRONG output under `karac run` (a=0 b=0 c=0) and a hard codegen error under `karac build` ('Binary op Add: left operand PointerType'). Root causes + fixes across both backends, A/B verified, Linux-LSan clean. INTERPRETER (2f0a7de1): `or_insert`/`or_insert_with` returned a DETACHED CLONE of the value, so scalar write-through silently dropped (heap Vec values only worked by accident via Arc-shared element storage). Fix: new `Value::MapSlotRef{map_var, key}` — a genuine place-ref into the live Map slot. `or_insert`/`or_insert_with` insert-if-absent then return it; `Env::get` resolves it to the slot value (auto-deref) and `Env::set` writes through to the slot, the SAME get/set choke-point treatment as `SharedCell`, so `*r += 1`, `r += 1` (deref-elided), `*r = v`, and `.push(x)` (Arc-shared elem) all land in the map. The bare-chain `*m.entry(k).or_insert(d) += 1` writes through via assign_to_place (Deref of a non-identifier yielding a MapSlotRef) + the Deref read resolves it in eval_unary; a MapSlotRef method receiver resolves to the slot value. Map SLOTS never hold a ref (only a local binding / chain-temp), so all map reads stay pristine (no collapse needed). Exhaustive arms: value.rs Display/variant_name (PartialEq + value_compare + deep_clone use catch-alls). CODEGEN (45089125): three gaps. (1) `*m.entry(k).or_insert(d) += rhs`: the entry chain lowers to a slot pointer WITH insert side effects, so compile it EXACTLY once then load/apply/store back through it (CompoundAssign + Assign deref cases in stmts.rs via `entry_chain_or_insert_map_name`). Previously `compile_expr(Deref(chain))` returned the raw pointer → binop PointerType error. (2) Two-step `let r = m.entry(k).or_insert(d); *r += 1`: bind `r` to the slot pointer + tag in NEW `entry_slot_ref_vars` (codegen analog of MapSlotRef; saved/restored across mono like ref_params, cleared per-fn) so `*r` reads / `*r += 1` / `r += 1` / `*r = v` write through (exprs.rs Deref read + stmts.rs assign/compound via `entry_slot_ref_target`/`entry_slot_ref_ptr`). Also closed a value-represented mut-ref deref-store: `*v += 1` in an `and_modify` closure SILENTLY dropped before (the `match target.kind` had no Deref(Identifier) arm); the canonical `v += 1` form already worked. (3) `Map.get_or(k, default)` was UNIMPLEMENTED in codegen ('not yet implemented'); lowered as a keyed lookup with a phi between the stored value and the default. The hit path returns an OWNED copy (deep-clone for String/Vec/struct V via emit_clone_fn_for_type_expr; rc_inc for shared V; load for scalar) because `get_or -> V` (owned), not a borrow — a raw alias double-freed (exit 133) with the map's drop at the caller's scope exit. KEY-TEMP LEAK (200b689c, caught by the Linux LSan gate — macOS ASAN misses reachable leaks): `karac_map_entry` bit-copies the key header into the bucket ONLY on the vacant insert (adopting the buffer); on the occupied path it keeps the existing key, so a fresh-owned-temp key (`m.entry(w.to_string())`) is orphaned — the counter leaked one key buffer per REPEATED key. `get_or` (any lookup) never stores its key. Fix: free the fresh-temp key when the runtime did not adopt it — entry chain frees conditionally on `occupied` (unconditionally for the `and_modify` lookup-only variant), `get_or` frees after the lookup; both via `free_fresh_owned_str_arg` (no-ops on borrowed cap==0 / non-Vec-String / non-fresh-temp). Moved-binding + place-expr keys on the no-adopt path remain a broader PRE-EXISTING map-key-ownership gap shared by get/insert (out of scope). Tests: interpreter 4 (direct counter, two-step mut-ref, per-key-Vec push, snapshot-not-ref); codegen 3 (direct counter, two-step mut-ref, get_or hit/default); memory_sanitizer 2 (entry counter + heap-valued get_or owned-copy, ≥36-byte payloads). Suites green: interpreter 976, codegen 1697, typechecker 1739, effectchecker 373, memory_sanitizer 304 (macOS ASAN) + Linux LSan map gate 27. NO new runtime symbol, NO archive rebuild.

Map-key NO-ADOPT ownership residual (the broader gap B-2026-06-20-8 deferred): the fresh-temp-only key free missed every non-fresh-temp owned key on a map's no-adopt path. `karac_map_entry` ADOPTS the key's {ptr,len,cap} buffer (bit-copies the header into the bucket) ONLY on the VACANT insert; the `and_modify` lookup variant, `get`/`get_or`/`remove`/`contains_key`, and `insert` on the EXISTS path never adopt. Three distinct bugs, one root cause (entry adopting like `insert` but not running insert's consume-site dance), confirmed via the Linux LSan gate (macOS ASAN misses the leaks but caught the entry vacant double-free). (1) ENTRY DOUBLE-FREE + occupied leak: a moved local binding / owned param / place key into `m.entry(k)` DOUBLE-FREED on the VACANT path (map adopts the buffer AND the un-suppressed source binding's scope-exit free releases the same buffer -- entry, unlike insert, never suppressed the source) and LEAKED on the OCCUPIED path (the B-2026-06-20-8 free was gated to fresh temps via expr_yields_fresh_owned_temp, so an Identifier/place key was untouched). Fix (entry_chains.rs emit_entry_chain): mirror Map.insert's key consume-site dance -- suppress_fstr_acc_if_moved_out + maybe_defensive_copy_param_arg (deep-copy an owned String/Vec PARAM key so the bucket owns a private buffer; the caller still frees the original under the by-value header ABI) + suppress_source_vec_cleanup_for_arg (zero a moved local binding's cap so its scope-exit free skips the adopted buffer); then free the key buffer on the NO-ADOPT branch (occupied for or_insert / always for the and_modify lookup variant), now UNGATED -- a vec-struct compile-time gate restricts the emitted free-diamond to String/Vec keys and the cap>0 runtime guard no-ops on a borrowed view / rodata literal. key_val is loaded before the suppress so the bucket still receives the owned (cap>0) header. (2) get/remove/contains_key FRESH-TEMP key leak: each looks the key up and never stores it, but only `get_or` freed its fresh-temp key -- so `m.get(w.to_string())` / remove / contains_key leaked one buffer per call. Fix: add free_fresh_owned_str_arg(&args[0].value, key_val) after the lookup in get / remove / contains_key (moved bindings are covered by their own un-suppressed source free, so the fresh-temp gate is correct here). (3) insert EXISTS-path key leak: insert suppresses the key source and adopts on a FRESH insert, but on the EXISTS (duplicate-key) path karac_map_insert_old keeps the bucket's existing key and never adopts the incoming one -- so the suppressed source's buffer is orphaned (one buffer per duplicate insert). Fix (maps.rs insert arm): free the owned incoming key (key_val, Some only on the non-borrowed path) on the existed (Some(old)) branch. Refactor: extracted the cap>0-guarded {ptr,len,cap} free out of free_fresh_owned_str_arg into the reusable, compile-time-gate-free free_str_vec_buffer_if_heap (runtime.rs); free_fresh_owned_str_arg keeps its fresh-temp expression gate and delegates the actual free -- behavior-preserving for all existing callers. NO runtime change, NO archive rebuild, NO interpreter change (the interpreter is GC-by-Value-clone; A/B output is byte-identical -- verified live: counter 3/2/1, get 3, entry +10 -> 12, contains_key has-cherry, identical under karac run and the codegen binary). Tests: 10 LSan regression tests in tests/memory_sanitizer.rs (entry moved-binding vacant no-double-free + occupied no-leak, entry owned-param vacant+occupied, entry and_modify moved-binding, get moved-binding + get fresh-temp, remove/contains fresh-temp [remove targets an ABSENT key on purpose -- see below], insert moved-binding-dup + owned-param-dup, insert duplicate heap-value discarded). All 24 map asan tests pass the Linux LSan gate; full worktree gate 314 passed / 3 failed where the 3 are PRE-EXISTING non-map leaks unrelated to this change (asan_owned_struct_option_shared_field_captured_from_builder_no_uaf, asan_single_field_struct_option_payload_sizing_no_bad_access, asan_string_eq_mismatched_len_no_overread_in_indexed_payload_match -- confirmed failing on clean main; from the recent selfhost codegen slices, invisible to the macOS post-landing run that has no LeakSanitizer). clippy --all --all-targets --features llvm clean, fmt clean. KNOWN OPEN (distinct, tracked separately): a present-key `remove` of a HEAP key tombstones the bucket WITHOUT freeing its STORED key (and the bool karac_map_remove variant also leaks the stored value) -- karac_map_free_with_drop_vec only frees OCCUPIED slots, so tombstoned buffers are orphaned. A full fix needs a drop-flag ABI on karac_map_remove_old (the runtime can't tell a heap key from a scalar one) + an archive rebuild -- out of scope for this codegen-only PR.

fix: Completes the map-key-ownership class B-2026-06-20-9 (c7b72bd4) started — that fix handled the key ARGUMENT (incoming, no-adopt); this handles the key/value already STORED in the bucket. A present-key remove of a HEAP key tombstones the bucket, but karac_map_free_with_drop_vec only walks OCCUPIED slots, so the tombstoned {ptr,len,cap} String/Vec key buffer is orphaned at map drop (Linux-LSan-only; macOS ASAN misses reachable leaks). FIXED via a drop-flag ABI on the runtime remove entry points + codegen threading the key's heap-ness through. RUNTIME (runtime/src/map.rs): karac_map_remove_old(map,key,out_old_val) gained drop_key: i32 — on a present-key tombstone it frees the bucket's STORED key when drop_key!=0 (heap {ptr,len,cap}) and cap>0; it NEVER frees the value, which is MOVED OUT to the caller via out_old_val (the returned Some(old) owns it — freeing it would double-free). The bool karac_map_remove(map,key) gained drop_key: i32, drop_val: i32 and frees BOTH stored halves (it discards both; the presence boolean carries no payload) — kept correct for the exported ABI even though codegen does not wire it (Map.remove/Set.remove lower to remove_old). Extracted the cap>0-guarded {ptr,len,cap} free into KaracMap::free_heap_field + free_stored_key/free_stored_val helpers and routed karac_map_free_with_drop_vec's live-slot walk through them (behavior-preserving dedup). CODEGEN: bumped the karac_map_remove_old declaration to 4 params (src/codegen.rs) and passed drop_key=llvm_ty_is_vec_struct(key_ty) at BOTH call sites — Map.remove (src/codegen/maps.rs) and Set.remove (src/codegen/collections.rs; elem_ty is the set's key, Set lowers to Map[T,()] so there is no value half). The Map.remove arm's existing incoming-key free (free_fresh_owned_str_arg, B-2026-06-20-9) is unchanged and complementary: it releases the key ARGUMENT on the no-adopt path; the new drop_key releases the STORED key the bucket adopted at insert. NO interpreter change (GC-by-Value-clone; A/B byte-identical). This MODIFIES an existing symbol's signature, so the prebuilt staticlib archives were REBUILT in the worktree (lean->min->full), not copied. TESTS: 2 LSan regression tests in tests/memory_sanitizer.rs (>=36-byte keys per the LSan-reachability rule) — asan_map_remove_present_heap_key_no_leak (Map[String,i64].remove(present)->Some; stored String key freed) and asan_map_remove_present_heap_key_and_vec_value_no_leak (Map[String,Vec[i64]].remove(present)->Some(vec): stored String key freed AND Vec value moved out + freed exactly once by the match-arm, NOT double-freed). NEGATIVE CONTROL: disabling the remove_old key free makes both tests FAIL under the Linux LSan gate (44 / 42 bytes leaked, exactly 1 allocation each = the stored key buffer; the value-test's single-alloc leak confirms the moved-out Vec is not leaked). GATES (worktree): fmt clean; clippy --all --all-targets --features llvm -D warnings clean; runtime unit tests 261; codegen 1700; macOS memory_sanitizer 319; Linux LSan gate 316 passed / 3 failed where the 3 are the PRE-EXISTING non-map leaks documented in B-2026-06-20-9 (asan_owned_struct_option_shared_field_captured_from_builder_no_uaf, asan_single_field_struct_option_payload_sizing_no_bad_access, asan_string_eq_mismatched_len_no_overread_in_indexed_payload_match — confirmed failing on clean main, unrelated to maps); all 39 map LSan tests green incl. the 2 new ones. KNOWN OPEN (distinct, out of scope): Set.remove of a heap element still leaks the INCOMING element — the B-2026-06-20-9 incoming-key class was never applied to Set (collections.rs has no free_fresh_owned_str_arg); flagged for a follow-up slice.
title: Present-key Map.remove / Set.remove of a HEAP key leaks the bucket's STORED key buffer (and the bool karac_map_remove variant leaks the stored value too).
