CORRECT STRATEGY (for the next attempt — do NOT retry deep-clone-on-bind for shared payloads): clone/inc-ON-EXTRACT, mirroring the EXISTING `share_bare_shared_ctor_payload` (call_dispatch.rs:4229), which rc-incs a bare shared handle at the CONSUME site (null-guarded, `rhs_yields_fresh_ref`-skipped) and is deliberately SCOPED (index-only) to avoid double-counting against the move/consumer-inc paths. Apply that shape: keep the shared-enum struct payload a VIEW (NO bind-time change -> zero interaction with the 17 shallow-view asan tests), and at the point a heap child is EXTRACTED/moved OUT of the view — the struct-destructure-let (`let CallNode { .. } = c`) and the field-access-move (`eval(b.left)`) — rc-INC each extracted shared child / deep-copy each extracted BUFFER child, so each escaping child is independently owned and its OWN consume/drop balances the extract inc (exactly how `v[i]` already works). This aligns with the rc-accounting instead of fighting the drop-suppression. Implementation surface: (1) mark shared-enum-payload view bindings (a per-binding flag/set — none exists today, only the per-match `pattern_binding_scrutinee_is_shared_enum`); (2) intercept the two extract sites for such sources. The copy-supported half (buffer-only payloads, commit ec23bc1) stays correct and is UNAFFECTED by this redirection — it works precisely because buffer-transfer survives suppression. The render use-after-free remains a separate third bug. 3 parser oracles stay #[ignore]'d. CLONE-ON-EXTRACT LANDED 2026-07-10 (destructure case — the redirected strategy WORKS): the root-cause redirection was implemented and VERIFIED. Instead of deep-clone-on-bind (which leaks shared incs against the move-out drop-suppression), the shared-enum struct payload stays a VIEW and each heap child is DUPLICATED at the point it is moved OUT via `let S { .. } = <view>` destructure. Implementation: (1) new state map `shared_enum_payload_view_vars` (codegen.rs, cleared per-fn in functions.rs) records a shared-enum struct-payload view binding + its type, populated at the view bind in pattern_binding.rs (the non-deep-cloned else branch); (2) `finish_owned_struct_destructure` (stmts.rs) gains a `view_src` branch calling the new `clone_on_extract_view_field`, which per-leaf DUPLICATES: a bare `shared` child -> rc-INC + track_rc_var (the leaf's consume/move balances the inc, the box's rc-drop balances its original ref — CRITICALLY this works because the LET-path `bind_pattern` does NOT pre-inc/track a shared leaf, unlike the match-value path, so there is NO double-count; and the existing shared-var move-suppression correctly disarms the scope-exit dec when the leaf is consumed — verified by asan); a String / `Vec[heap-free elem]` -> buffer deep-copy + track; a nested `struct_clone_fully_duplicates` struct -> deep_copy + track; (3) the `Option[struct/enum]` destructure path is guarded `&& !view_src` so it does not track an aliased view Option. Reused `rc_inc_shared_handle_at_slot` (null-guarded). Gates: memory_sanitizer 577 GREEN (+1 regression asan_shared_enum_view_destructure_bare_shared_child — nested BinNode with bare-shared left/right destructured + recursively consumed, looped x20 under LSan), codegen 2120, fmt + clippy clean; the bare-shared view-destructure repro is interp==compiled. RESIDUAL (parser still crashes — malloc unaligned tcache — so oracles STAY #[ignore]'d): `clone_on_extract_view_field` deliberately BAILS (keeps status-quo view alias) on `Vec[shared]` / `Vec[String|agg]` / `Option` / `Map` / `Set` LEAF kinds, and the whole mechanism only covers the `let S { .. } = view` DESTRUCTURE — NOT the FIELD-ACCESS-MOVE (`eval(b.left)`, `let tb = nd.then_block`), which is the other move-out form the parser uses (owned_struct_params already has a precedent field-access deep-copy at B-2026-06-10-2 to extend). NEXT STEPS, in order: (a) extend `clone_on_extract_view_field` to `Vec[shared]` (deep-copy buffer + per-element rc-inc — the element loop shape is in `deep_copy_vec_aggregate_elements_in_place`; the SEGV in the earlier param-path attempt was from applying it on the PARAM path, which this view-scoped path avoids) and to `Option[shared]` (rc-inc Some via `deep_copy_option_inline_payload_in_place` + the inline-option track); (b) add the FIELD-ACCESS-MOVE interception for view vars (mirror the B-2026-06-10-2 owned_struct_params field-access deep-copy, adding the bare-shared rc-inc leg); (c) then the render use-after-free. Each step asan-gated. Landed increments so far: ec23bc1 (copy-supported bind) + this commit (bare-shared destructure). CLONE-ON-EXTRACT COMPLETE for intra-function view move-out 2026-07-10 (5 increments landed) + RENDER-UAF ISOLATED as the remaining blocker. The redirected clone-on-extract strategy is now applied comprehensively to every intra-function move-out of a shared-enum-payload VIEW: DESTRUCTURE form (`let S { .. } = view`) covers bare-`shared` (rc-inc + track_rc_var), String / Vec[heap-free], `Vec[shared]` (buffer deep-copy + per-element rc-inc via rc_inc_vec_shared_elements + per-element rc-dec drop), `Option[shared]` (rc-inc Some + track_rc_option_var), and nested clone-duplicable structs; FIELD-ACCESS-MOVE form covers `let a = view.vecfield` for `Vec[shared]` (deep_copy_owned_struct_param_field_move extended to view sources) — bare-`shared` and `Option[shared]` field-moves were already balanced by compile_field_access's read-inc. Commits: ec23bc1 (copy-supported bind), 96271ee (bare-shared destructure), 56327fb (Vec/Option-shared destructure), 4f99c60 (Vec[shared] field-move). All asan-green (memory_sanitizer 579, +4 regression tests) + codegen 2120 + fmt/clippy. The self-hosted parser now renders EVERY simple expression correctly (literals, idents, unary, binary, call, field — verified by building selfhost/src and driving parse_and_print). REMAINING BLOCKER — the RENDER USE-AFTER-FREE (the distinct 'second nested-drop bug' the original entry flagged, now isolated): `{ a; }` renders as `(block .. (exprstmt @2:1 (error @1:1)))` — the `ExprStmt.expr` (a shared Expr handle) reads back as a garbage Error node while its inline `span` survives (classic UAF: shared handle freed, scalar intact). ROOT CAUSE is CROSS-FUNCTION, not intra-function: `render_expr` matches `Blk(block)` binding `block` as a Block VIEW, then passes it BY VALUE to `render_block(b: Block)`. Block is NOT copy-supported (shared `Option[Expr]` tail + `Vec[Stmt]` whose Stmt wraps a shared Expr), so `make_aggregate_param_callee_owned` bails and `b` is a CALLER-RETAINS shallow alias of the box's payload (registered in `owned_struct_params`). render_block then `let Block { stmts, tail, span } = b` destructures and CONSUMES the shared contents (`for s in stmts { render_stmt(s) }`, each `render_stmt`/`render_expr` consuming a shared Expr by value → rc-dec), decrementing the box's children while the box (`render_expr`'s consumed `e`) still owns them → premature free / double-dec. The intra-function view map (`shared_enum_payload_view_vars`) does NOT propagate across the call, so render_block's `b` is not recognized as a view and its destructure aliases rather than duplicates. NEXT STEP (distinct, harder slice): either (a) make a shared-enum-payload view passed BY VALUE to a non-copy-supported struct param deep-duplicate at the CALL SITE (propagate view-ness / duplicate the view before the call), or (b) treat a caller-retains struct param whose fields transitively own shared handles as needing clone-on-extract on destructure in the CALLEE (extend the view map to such params). Both are cross-function ownership work — a focused fresh slice. selfhost oracles STAY #[ignore]'d; main is green. RENDER-UAF ROOT-CAUSED 2026-07-10 (fix identified, NOT landable yet — trades UAF for a leak): the render use-after-free is NOT the cross-function view issue previously hypothesized — it reproduces with a DIRECT owned Block (no view). Minimal repro: `shared enum Expr { Lit(IntLit), Blk(Block) }`, `struct ExprStmt { expr: Expr, span }`, `enum Stmt { Exp(ExprStmt) }`, `struct Block { stmts: Vec[Stmt], span }`; `render_block(b: Block)` by value destructures + per-stmt consumes. ROOT CAUSE: Block is COPY-SUPPORTED (field_copy_supported admits `Vec[Stmt]` unconditionally), so `b` is CALLEE-OWNED (entry deep-copied via deep_copy_struct_heap_fields_in_place). The entry-copy recurses `Vec[Stmt]` -> deep_copy_vec_aggregate_elements -> ElemCopy::Enum(Stmt) -> deep_copy_enum_heap_payload_in_place -> (NestedStruct arm) deep_copy_struct_heap_fields_in_place(ExprStmt) -> deep_copy_one_aggregate_field(expr: Expr) — but `expr` is a BARE `shared` field and deep_copy_one_aggregate_field has NO bare-shared arm, so the callee-owned copy SHALLOW-ALIASES the Expr handle; the copy's drop AND the caller's drop both rc-dec it -> UAF (in the renderer, ExprStmt.expr reads back as a garbage Error node while its inline span survives). THE FIX (add a bare-shared rc-INC arm to deep_copy_one_aggregate_field) makes the minimal repros CORRECT (interp==compiled, UAF gone) and the FULL asan suite stays green EXCEPT it introduces a LEAK (1920 B / 40 allocs = all the Expr boxes) in the specific shape where the element is MOVED, not retained: `for s in stmts` MOVES each Stmt into render_stmt (also callee-owned), whose entry-copy rc-INCs expr again, but the moved-out source doesn't retain a ref to rc-DEC -> the inc is unbalanced. This is the FRESH/MOVED-vs-RETAINED entry-copy distinction (the #17-gap family): make_aggregate_param_callee_owned entry-copies UNCONDITIONALLY, but a MOVED aggregate arg is already owned by the callee and must NOT be inc'd. The bare-shared arm was REVERTED to keep main green (a leak is LSan-CI-red; the pre-existing UAF is the known-open status quo). NEXT STEP: gate the entry-copy bare-shared inc (and by extension the whole entry-copy) on the arg being RETAINED, not a fresh/moved temp — i.e. skip the entry-copy inc for a moved-in aggregate (it already owns its ref), OR balance it with a dec at the move site. This is the same fresh-vs-retained gap #17 handles for buffers, extended to nested shared handles. SEPARATELY: with the bare-shared arm applied, a faithful minimal render repro (Block with tail:Option[Expr] + Span struct + String ident) PASSES, yet the real self-hosted parser STILL emits `(exprstmt @2:1 (error @1:1))` for `{ a; }` — so there is ALSO a distinct parse-time or deeper-shape issue in the actual parser beyond this entry-copy UAF, still to isolate. The 5 landed clone-on-extract increments (ec23bc1/96271ee/56327fb/4f99c60 + this session's earlier work) stay; memory_sanitizer 579 green; parser oracles stay #[ignore]'d. SPLIT 2026-07-10: the `{ a; }` -> `(error @1:1)` render corruption that THIS entry repeatedly attributed to a render/nested-drop USE-AFTER-FREE is, on isolation, NOT a UAF and NOT part of this bug — it is a DETERMINISTIC codegen WRONG-VALUE miscompile (interp!=compiled, exit 0, no heap corruption; the statement-expr field of a `Vec[Stmt]` reads back as a bogus `Error` node while the tail `Option[Expr]` is correct). Filed as its own id B-2026-07-10-1 (surface codegen, class miscompile). This entry (B-2026-07-09-12) is the MEMORY-SAFETY family only — the entry-copy rc-inc / clone-on-extract / nested-drop double-free work whose failures CRASH (exit 139). The remaining open work HERE is the render-UAF fresh-vs-retained entry-copy fix; the parser oracle needs BOTH this AND B-2026-07-10-1 green before it can be un-#[ignore]'d. RENDER-UAF DOUBLE-ENTRY-COPY LEAK CONFIRMED via IR 2026-07-10 (after the B-2026-07-10-1 fix): re-applying the entry-copy bare-`shared` arm (deep_copy_one_aggregate_field rc-incs a nested bare shared field) NOW fixes the render2 UAF AND keeps the WHOLE existing memory_sanitizer suite green (580) — the B-2026-07-10-1 `len`-zeroing removed the whole-struct-move variant of the old leak. BUT a NEW regression test for the render_block shape (a copy-supported struct passed by value whose Vec[Stmt] elements are consumed via a for-loop into ANOTHER callee-owned fn render_stmt) still LEAKS (1920 B / 40 = all the Expr boxes), and the real parser oracle CORRUPTS ('malloc unsorted double linked list'). IR diff of render_block + render_stmt: BOTH entry-copy their struct param and rc-INC the same nested Expr handle (`p14.shf` / `viewdup.inc` in each), but the value flows by MOVE (render_block's b.stmts element is MOVED into render_stmt via the for-loop). So there are TWO entry-copy incs on one logical owner. render_block's entry-inc is meant to be balanced by its `b` scope-exit combined-drop's LEN-driven element walk, but the for-loop CONSUMES b.stmts out first (the walk skips) → that inc LEAKS; render_stmt's own entry-inc + render_expr consume-dec net zero. ROOT: `make_aggregate_param_callee_owned` entry-copies UNCONDITIONALLY, but a MOVED aggregate arg (a for-loop element, a fresh temp) is ALREADY owned by the callee and must NOT be re-copied/re-inc'd — the fresh-vs-retained distinction (#17-gap family), NESTED-call variant. The arm was NOT landed (pushed once as af97e03, reverted 948d552 the moment the new test flagged the leak). The clean fix is to SKIP the callee entry-copy (and its shared-inc) for an arg known to be moved/fresh — e.g. a for-loop-owned element (`for_loop_owned_agg_vars`) or an `expr_yields_fresh_owned_temp` arg — which needs the call-site's move/retain knowledge threaded to the callee-owned-param entry-copy (a cross-boundary change). This is the SOLE remaining blocker for B-2026-07-09-12; the double-free/UAF half is understood and the arm fixes it — only the moved-arg double-inc leak remains. Two other parser bugs from this investigation are already CLOSED: B-2026-07-10-1 (stmt-expr miscompile) and the intra-function clone-on-extract family (6 commits). SHARP RE-LOCALIZATION 2026-07-10 (residual parser crash is a CONSTRUCTION wild-write, DISTINCT from the render-UAF/move-out family above; NO code landed, main green at 5603620): on current main the self-hosted parser BUILDS, renders every simple expression correctly, and renders `{ a; }` correctly (B-2026-07-10-1 landed). The ignored `selfhost_parser` oracle still fails because the parser SEGVs (exit 139) on exactly 8 of the 150 corpus inputs — ALL of them `if`/`loop`/`for`/`while` with block bodies (`if a { b } else { c }`, `if a { b } else { }`, `loop { if done { break } }`, `{ for x in xs { f(x) } }`, `{ let r = if a { b } else { c }; r }`, ...). Bisected in the REAL parser: crash needs an `else` branch AND a non-empty THEN-block tail (`if a { } else { }` and `if a { b }` are BOTH clean; only `if a { b } else {..}` crashes). MINIMAL STANDALONE REPRO (no ast.kara needed): a `shared enum` variant carrying a STRUCT payload that has ALL THREE of [a bare `shared` field, a NESTED-STRUCT field that itself holds an `Option[shared]`, and an `Option[shared]` field] — i.e. `IfExpr { cond: Expr, then_block: Block, else_branch: Option[Expr], span }` with `Block { stmts: Vec[Stmt], tail: Option[Expr], span }`, built via `Expr.If(IfExpr { .. })` where `else_branch` comes from a FUNCTION RETURN (sret). Removing ANY ONE of the three fields makes it CLEAN (`cond+else`, `cond+then_block`, `then_block+else` all pass); `Expr.Ident(..)` and `Expr.Blk(Block)` (single nested-struct payload) are CLEAN. So the trigger is the 3-field COMBINATION, not payload size or the fn-return alone (a 3-variant enum with the same shape does NOT crash — needs the fuller Expr so the variant is packed a certain way). ROOT (gdb + asan, `KARAC_OPT_LEVEL=0` for IR-faithful trace): the crash is DURING `Expr.If(..)` construction, an `incq (%rN)` rc-INCREMENT of a handle whose value is literally `1` (`SEGV WRITE to address 0x1`) — an `Option`s Some-tag word (=1) being treated as a shared POINTER and inc'd. The `Expr` box is 152 bytes (rc8 + tag8 + 17 INLINE payload words; the IfExpr payload is packed INLINE, not boxed), tag 19 stored at box+8, and the bad `1` is stored at a payload word AND inc'd. Crashes even with `else_branch = None` (the FIELD LAYOUT, not the runtime value) — so a nested `Option[shared]`s tag word is mis-located into a shared-handle slot during the INLINE shared-enum-variant payload packing / per-field capture-inc. FIX SURFACE (next session): the INLINE shared-enum-variant construction capture path — `build_shared`/`build_nonshared` in call_dispatch.rs + `coerce_to_payload_words` + the per-field shared-handle capture (`emit_rc_inc_for_captured_option` / bare-shared `apply_shared_transfer`) — mis-computes the word offset of an `Option[shared]` inner pointer when a nested-struct field precedes it in the payload; the individual captures read correct LOCALS in `compile_to_ir` (which BOXES this payload!), so the divergence is that `karac build` INLINES it while `compile_to_ir` boxed — reconcile the inline/box decision and the inline nested-Option offset. SEPARATE (likely a leak, not the crash): `__karac_drop_struct_Block` drops ONLY field 0 (`stmts`) then `ret void` — it NEVER drops field 1 (`tail: Option[shared]`); its sibling `__karac_vec_elem_full_drop_Block` adds the tail drop but DOUBLE-DROPS the `stmts` elements (once via `__karac_drop_struct_Block`s internal per-elem loop, again via its own `nstr.vec` loop). `__karac_rc_drop_Expr`s If-walk is correct+complete. TECHNIQUES that worked (reuse next time): (1) drive the parser PER-INPUT by making its `main` read `Env.args()` and passing one corpus string per process invocation — build ONCE, loop the corpus in the shell; batching all 150 in one `main` gives nondeterministic heap-corruption messages (`unsorted double linked list` / `unaligned tcache`) that mask WHICH input crashes. (2) asan a multi-file `karac build` project by TEMPORARILY reading `KARAC_EXTRA_CC_ARGS` in `driver.rs::link_executable_exports` and building with `-fsanitize=address -g` (the runtime archive is not asan-instrumented, but the malloc/free interceptors catch the wild write). (3) `KARAC_OPT_LEVEL=0` makes the binary map ~1:1 to `compile_to_ir` output for gdb. Oracles STAY #[ignore]d; main is green; no code changed this session. ROOT CAUSE FOUND + FIXED 2026-07-10 (the parser SEGV was an AUTO-PAR CORRECTNESS bug, NOT a construction/drop memory bug): the residual crash on every control-flow expression (`if`/`loop`/`for`/`while`) was the auto-parallelizer FALSELY PARALLELIZING sequential `mut ref self` calls. `parse_if` compiles `let cond = self.parse_expr_bp(0); let then_block = self.parse_block(); let else_branch = self.parse_else();` into a 3-branch `karac_par_run` group — but those three calls all take `mut ref self` and sequentially advance the SAME parser cursor (`self.pos`), so they have a strict ordering dependency and MUST run serially. Racing them corrupts parser state, producing garbage AST handles that the subsequent `Expr.If(..)` construction rc-incs (the `incq` of a `0x1` Some-tag value the earlier note chased). CORRECTION: the prior `SHARP RE-LOCALIZATION` note (construction wild-write / inline shared-enum packing offset / 3-field trigger) was a RED HERRING — the `0x1` handle was garbage produced by the raced parse, not a packing-offset bug; the single-file repros never crashed because they were not auto-parallelized (free-function calls / no shared `mut ref self`). Confirmed by `KARAC_AUTO_PAR=0` making all 8 crashing corpus inputs parse correctly. ROOT in `src/concurrency.rs`: (1) `method_effects_imply_receiver_mutation` only flagged receiver mutation via non-pure RESOURCE-effect verbs (`writes`/`allocates`/...), but a cursor advance (`self.pos = self.pos + 1`) is plain-field mutation with NO resource effect, so the three calls recorded no write on `self`; (2) the `StmtKind::Let` arm collected the RHS's reads + effects but NEVER its inner-WRITES, so even a flagged receiver mutation wouldn't reach `info.defines`. FIX: added `method_receiver_is_mut_ref` (a `mut ref self` receiver CAN mutate through the borrow, independent of effect verbs) OR-composed into the MethodCall write-collection, and made the `Let`/`LetElse` arms call `collect_expr_inner_writes(value, &mut info.defines)` (mirroring the `Expr` arm). Now `let x = self.mut_method()` records `self` as written → the three calls conflict (write-write on `self`) → serialized → no par group in `parse_if`. Gates: concurrency 110 (+1 regression `test_mut_ref_self_calls_serialize`), full non-llvm 7590, par_codegen 155, memory_sanitizer 584, codegen 2127, fmt+clippy clean. The `selfhost_parser` (expression) oracle is UN-IGNORED and passes (150/150 corpus). REMAINING (separate bug, filed B-2026-07-10-3): the `selfhost_parser_items`/`_types` oracles still crash — but that is a DISTINCT render-time `free()` double-free in `render_item`'s drop walk (heap corruption, NOT auto-par: fails with `KARAC_AUTO_PAR=0` too), a residual of the original nested-drop double-free family on the wider item AST (FnDefNode / EnumDefNode / TraitDefNode). B-2026-07-09-12 (the control-flow-expression parser SEGV) is CLOSED by this commit.

fix: this commit. ROOT CAUSE was NOT struct-key-specific (the original entry misattributed it): the trigger is a `let` binding between a borrow-returning collection accessor and the consuming match — `let g = coll.get(k); match g { Some(v) => <move/drop v> }`. `Map.get` returns `Option[V]` whose payload ALIASES the bucket's stored value (and Vec/Slice/Array `.get`/`.first`/`.last` return `Option[ref T]` aliasing element storage); the codegen borrow-alias protection (`scrutinee_is_borrow_call` + `clone_escaping_borrow_payload_binding`) only recognized a DIRECT `match coll.get(k)` method-call scrutinee. Through the intermediate `let g`, the scrutinee is an IDENTIFIER, so the protection was skipped: an arm that moved/dropped the payload freed the aliased buffer a second time against the collection's own value/element drop (`karac_map_free_with_drop_vec`). The struct-key repro in the original entry just happened to trip glibc's tcache double-free detector (ASan/valgrind confirmed the free came from `main`'s payload drop, not the hash path — which is correct); the identical i64-key `let`-form double-freed too, and Vec.get `let`-form was the same class. FIX: a new `borrow_accessor_let_payload: HashMap<var, Option-te>` records a `let` binding whose RHS is a borrow-returning stdlib-collection accessor (Map/SortedMap `.get`; Vec/Slice/Array/VecDeque `.get`/`.first`/`.last`) at the let-site (src/codegen/stmts.rs). `scrutinee_is_borrowed_binding` re-admits such an identifier scrutinee into the borrow protection, and `borrow_get_payload_clone_te` recovers the payload type for it — so the Map payload clones on escape exactly as in the direct form and the ref-typed Vec payload self-gates to alias-only. Cleared per-function alongside `for_loop_borrow_vars`. Tests: codegen test_ir_map_get_letbound_moveout_emits_payload_clone; memory_sanitizer asan_letbound_map_get_moveout_no_double_free (bare-String value + struct KEY + if-let sibling, looped). Full suites green (codegen 2112, memory_sanitizer 569, interpreter 1138, par_codegen 154); read-only map.get stays zero-cost (no clone). RESIDUAL (known limitation): the fix covers the common `let g = coll.get(k); match g`/`if let` idiom, but the alias model is still fragile through OTHER indirections — passing `g` to a function, returning it, or chained rebinds (`let h = g; match h`) — because the borrow property is only propagated one hop. These are rarer (the ergonomic path is to match at the call site or `.clone()`). The principled long-term fix is to RETYPE `Map.get -> Option[ref V]` (like `Vec.get`) so the typechecker REJECTS escapes and forces an explicit `.clone()` to own — aligning the type with the codegen reality (the payload IS a borrow) and retiring the whole clone-on-escape patch family. That is an API/semantics decision (touches typechecker + interpreter + all Map.get usage), deferred to a design discussion rather than a bug-fix slice.
title: [root cause corrected — see fix] A struct-KEYED `Map[S, String]` (S a `#[derive(Hash, Eq, PartialEq)]` struct) double-frees a String VALUE under codegen (`karac build`/JIT) on the get-after-multi-insert path; the interpreter runs the same program correctly. Minimal repro: `#[derive(Hash, Eq, PartialEq)] struct Point { x: i64, y: i64 } fn main() { let mut m: Map[Point, String] = Map.new(); m.insert(Point{x:1,y:2}, "a".to_string()); m.insert(Point{x:3,y:4}, "b".to_string()); match m.get(Point{x:1,y:2}) { Some(v) => println(v), None => println("miss") } }` -> `karac run --interp` prints `a`; JIT/AOT aborts `free(): double free detected in tcache 2`. ISOLATION: the double-free needs ALL of (struct key) + (heap/String VALUE) + (>=2 inserts) + (a get). Narrowing that flips it back to clean: `Map[Point, i64]` (POD value, 2 inserts+get) is clean; `Map[Name{first:String}, i64]` (String-in-KEY, POD value) is clean; `Map[i64, String]` and `Map[String, String]` (non-struct key, String value, 2 inserts+get) are clean; struct-key + String value with ONLY 1 insert (no get) is clean; struct-key + String value with 2 inserts and NO get is clean. So the trigger is specifically a struct-key map's `get(...) -> Option[V]` when V is heap-owning and the table has >1 entry. LIKELY CAUSE: the struct-key `get` lowering returns the stored String VALUE by shallow copy (aliasing the bucket's {ptr,len,cap}) rather than an owned deep-copy / borrow, so the match-bound `v` and the map's own scope-exit drop free the same buffer twice -- the same ownership family as B-2026-06-20-8's `get_or` owned-copy fix, but on the `get`+struct-key path (the >1-entry / get combination suggests the struct-key hash bucket walk selects a value pointer the caller then adopts). The struct-KEY hash path ITSELF is correct (POD and String-field keys both hit the right bucket), so this is a VALUE-ownership bug surfaced by the struct-key get codepath, not a derive(Hash) miscompile. Found while surveying the roadmap `std.hash` item; NOTE for that survey: `#[derive(Hash)]` codegen for Map/Set keys already works (contra the roadmap's 'codegen path (interpreter form already shipped)' phrasing) -- the open `std.hash` work is the user-facing `Hash` trait + `Hasher` interface surface, not the Map-key byte-hash which ships.

PERF-REGRESSION (auto-par cost model, NO correctness impact): the default `karac build` (auto-par ON) fans out a ~70us-spawn parallel group in a hot function's PROLOGUE when it contains an empty-collection constructor, blowing the build up 40-66x vs its `KARAC_AUTO_PAR=0` seq lane, NON-DETERMINISTICALLY. `find_parallel_groups` marks a group trivial (-> sequential codegen, no spawn) when `all_pure || non_constant_count <= 1`, where non_constant_count counts statements `expr_is_constant_init` does NOT classify as zero-work. That predicate listed scalar/string/composite literals + identifiers but NOT empty-collection constructors -- yet an empty `Vec.new()` / `String.new()` only materializes a {ptr,len,cap} descriptor with no heap allocation (the first push/grow allocates; the allocates(Heap) effect is conservative for that later growth, which is also why all_pure is false). So a prologue like `let hexd=".."; let n = num & M; let buf = Vec.new();` (kata #405 to_hex) counts TWO non-constant stmts (the `& M` bitand AND `Vec.new()`) -> clears the `<= 1` gate -> emits a 3-branch par-block PER CALL. Over the millions of to_hex calls in #405's render loop the default build retires 370-608B (measured 503B) instructions vs 9.5B for the seq lane, with run-to-run variance characteristic of scheduler spin. Output is UNCHANGED (sink 2231199964 == seq), a pure perf regression. The kata's own bench.sh documents the workload 'does not auto-parallelize' (loop-carried buffer dep), so this silently violated that stated invariant.
investigation: SURFACED while taking B-2026-07-08-7 (the #405 allocator slice). Measure-first REFRAMED the premise: (1) the general 'kara allocator is 2.7x slower' gap does NOT reproduce -- a tiny-per-call Vec churn micro (2M allocs, push-loop both sides) runs kara 1.33B instr / 48ms vs rust 4.25B / 146ms, i.e. kara ~3x FASTER; (2) #405 to_hex single-threaded is 9.5B vs rust 9.2B instructions (~parity), 0.70s vs 0.58s wall (~1.21x, memory/IPC-bound), RSS 33MB < rust 36MB; (3) the documented #405 levers are all compiler-side -- escape-analysis elision (blocked, multi-day), SSO (in progress by a concurrent session, src/codegen/sso.rs stubbed, inline construction not yet on), and a push_char/push_byte fast path (which the roadmap claimed spec'd but does NOT exist on String today); a runtime free-list was already dead-ended (realloc no-win reverted, grow-floor +39% RSS). None move the ~1.21x wall gap, so no allocator slice was built. The ACTUAL high-value bug was this: default `karac build` of to_hex reads 40-66x its seq lane, non-deterministically, from auto-par spin -- root-caused via `--concurrency-report` to a parallel_group over [hexd literal][num & M][Vec.new()] in to_hex. FIX: `expr_is_constant_init` recognizes zero-arg `Vec.new()`/`String.new()` as constant-init, mirroring the byte-lit (kata-91, 2026-05-22) and ordered-output-probe (2026-06-14) precedents; drops non_constant_count to 1 -> trivial -> sequential, while main's legit outer `+` reduction still parallelizes (to_hex default build now 9.49B deterministic / 0.31s, FASTER than rust-ofl 0.58s single-core). Correct by construction: an empty constructor is never the work branch, so the fix only ever removes overhead-only fan-outs, never profitable parallelism. Map.new()/Set.new() EXCLUDED (may eagerly allocate a table). Also fixed en route: stale installed runtime archives (~/.local/lib + ~/.cargo/lib missing karac_alloc_zeroed_or_panic, breaking every Vec.filled(n,0) link) -- refreshed. Regression test is discriminating (2 non-constant stmts pre-fix -> fails; 1 post-fix -> passes). Gates: concurrency 108, par_codegen 154, parallax 6, parallax_lite 5, codegen E2E 2106; fmt + clippy (--all-targets --features llvm) clean.

tracker: runtime/src/map.rs (karac_map_try_insert + try_resize + alloc_storage_fallible); src/codegen/maps.rs (compile_map_method try_insert arm); src/codegen/collections.rs (compile_set_method try_insert arm)
title: CODEGEN-GAP (no correctness impact — interpreter worked, `karac build` rejected cleanly): `Map.try_insert` / `Set.try_insert` were interpreter-only, the last open leaf of the phase-8 fallible-allocation API split (item 8). `karac build` rejected them with the actionable 'interpreter-only; item 8' message. Blocked on a prereq the ledger flagged: the custom open-addressing `KaracMap` runtime (runtime/src/map.rs) grows via `std::alloc::alloc` with NO null check, so an insert that needs to resize aborts/segfaults on OOM rather than surfacing a recoverable failure — there was no fallible `karac_map_*` entry point for the `try_*` companions to lower onto. FIXED: implemented the fallible runtime path + codegen for Map and Set (SortedSet split out — see below). Contract (matches the `karac run` interpreter oracle): `Map.try_insert(k,v) -> Result[Option[V], AllocError]` (Ok(None) fresh, Ok(Some(old)) overwrite, Err(OutOfMemory{bytes}) on OOM); `Set.try_insert(k) -> Result[bool, AllocError]` (Ok(true) newly inserted, Ok(false) duplicate).
investigation: Taken as 'the remaining task' of the phase-8 fallible-alloc section (the sole open `[ ]` leaf). SCOPING established: (a) the runtime map is a custom `#[repr(C)] KaracMap` open-addressing table, NOT std HashMap; growth (`resize` -> `alloc_storage` -> `alloc`) is the ONLY allocation an insert performs (the slot write is copy-only), so making resize fallible makes the whole op fallible; (b) Set and SortedSet share the `is_set` codegen path, and mono `Map[i64,i64]` methods GEP the SAME `KaracMap` struct (offset-pinned by `karac_map_field_offsets_match_codegen`), so a single generic `karac_map_try_insert` covers mono + generic + Set; (c) `Map.insert` returns `Option[V]` (old value) and `Set.insert` returns `bool` (newly-inserted) — verified against the interpreter, which the codegen must match. RUNTIME (runtime/src/map.rs): `alloc_storage_fallible` (null-checks each `alloc`, frees any partial, returns None), `try_resize` (allocates new storage BEFORE mutating any `self` field, so OOM leaves the map completely unchanged with no rollback; returns Err(attempted_bytes)), and `karac_map_try_insert(map,key,val,out_old_val,out_failed_bytes) -> i32` (0 fresh / 1 updated / 2 OOM), the fallible sibling of `karac_map_insert_old`. Added to `__preserve_no_mangle_symbols` + a runtime unit test (fresh/update/growth across several resizes). CODEGEN: `compile_map_method` `try_insert` arm builds `Result[Option[V], AllocError]` reusing the panicking `insert` arm's `Option[V]` construction (`coerce_to_payload_words`+`build_option_some_via_phis`) wrapped in `Result.Ok`, with a status-2 branch to `build_alloc_oom_result`; `compile_set_method` `try_insert` arm builds `Result[bool, AllocError]`. Both use the generic pointer runtime path only (mono + borrowed-key fast paths are perf, orthogonal to fallibility). The duplicate-key / duplicate-element / overwrite-move-out no-adopt frees (B-2026-06-20-9/12 siblings) are mirrored on the fallible path so the single-drop contract holds. `insert` added to `CODEGEN_FALLIBLE_INSTANCE_BASES` so the item-2 build guard passes it through to dispatch (the guard test now exercises the still-blocked `Map.try_clone`). SortedSet.try_insert canNOT be built: SortedSet/SortedMap are ENTIRELY interpreter-only in codegen (even panicking `SortedSet.insert` falls through 'no handler for method'), so it is blocked on SortedSet getting any codegen backend — a separate larger slice, NOT a fallible-alloc gap; left open as its own checklist leaf. VERIFIED: 4 codegen E2E (Map[i64,i64] fresh/update/growth, Map[i64,String] heap-value Option packing, Set[i64], Map.try_insert `?`-propagation) byte-identical to `karac run`; 3 memory_sanitizer (heap-value overwrite move-out, heap-key duplicate no-adopt, Set[String] element duplicate no-adopt) green under LINUX ASAN+LSan via scripts/lsan-local.sh (macOS ASAN alone misses the leak class). Gates: codegen 2116, memory_sanitizer, interpreter 1138, typechecker 1938, effect 388; fmt + clippy (--all-targets --features llvm) clean.

FIXED: `SortedSet[T]` now lowers under `karac build`, sharing Set's `KaracMap`-backed storage for all order-independent ops and materializing ascending order only at the iteration + min/max observation points. Byte-identical to the `karac run` (BTreeMap) oracle for integer and String element types.
investigation: Picked as the next task after B-2026-07-09-15. SCOPING found this far more tractable than a new sorted-tree backend: (1) SortedSet.new already constructs a KaracMap (`build_map_new_handle_from_type_expr` treats Set|SortedSet identically); the gap was purely that the recognition helpers (`is_set_new_call`, `extract_set_elem_type`, `extract_set_elem_name`, `set_inner_type_expr`) gated on segment=='Set', so a SortedSet var never entered `set_elem_types` and every method fell through. (2) Most SortedSet methods (insert/contains/remove/len/is_empty/try_insert/union/intersection/difference) are ORDER-INDEPENDENT and reuse `compile_set_method` verbatim once the var is registered; only the ITERATION-producing ops (for-loop) and min/max need ordering. FIX: extend the four recognition helpers to accept 'SortedSet'; add a `sorted_collection_vars` HashSet marked from the `SortedSet[T]` annotation (so plain Set is untouched). RUNTIME: `karac_map_sorted_keys(map, out_len, cmp_fn) -> *mut u8` gathers live-key pointers, sorts them by the codegen-passed comparator (Vec::sort_by), and returns a malloc'd ascending copy (caller frees). CODEGEN: `emit_sorted_key_cmp_fn` emits a LinkOnceODR `karac_sortcmp_<T>(*const,*const)->i32` for integer (any width, +char/bool) and String keys (reusing the karac_string_cmp path, same surface as Vec.binary_search); `emit_sorted_keys_buf` wraps the call. The sorted for-loop (control_flow_for.rs) walks the buffer by index with the SAME borrow-like element binding as the hash path (so the freed header buffer never owns the aliased key data — String for-loops carry over) and frees the buffer at exit (free(NULL) safe for the empty set). SortedSet.min/max (compile_set_method) read buf[0]/buf[len-1], CLONE the picked element via `emit_clone_fn_for_type_expr` (so a returned Option[String] owns its payload independent of the freed buffer and the set's key), and free the buffer. VERIFIED byte-identical to `karac run`: ascending int + String for-loop, min/max, empty->None, dedup, and `let`-bound `union`; 2 codegen E2E (int, String) + 1 memory_sanitizer (String iter+min/max, exercising clone/borrow/free) green under LINUX ASAN+LSan (scripts/lsan-local.sh), not just macOS ASAN. No regression: codegen 2116, interpreter 1138, typechecker 1938, effect 388; fmt + clippy (--all-targets --features llvm) clean. SCOPE / follow-ons (own checklist leaf): SortedMap.* (the map sibling — same substrate + ordered keys/values/entries + get/entry); derived-Ord struct/enum elements (rejected at emit_sorted_key_cmp_fn, interpreter-only); an INLINE set-op iterable (`for x in a.union(b)` without a let-binding) is not yet marked sorted (the let-bound result is). Cross-ref [[B-2026-07-09-15]].
